Invite only: Senior test analyst

Tenders.Net #: 1602772
Agency: Australian Crime Commission
Location: ACT
Closing: 30/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06237 - 1 Senior Test Analyst
Job details
The Test analyst will be principally responsible for designing, reviewing and executing manual and automated test cases as required for multiple projects across different systems/service lines. The role will be responsible for developing test planning and reporting on deliverables, review test cases to ensure accuracy, completeness, traceability and coverage of business requirements and risks
Key duties and responsibilities
The principal accountabilities and responsibilities include:
• Design, review and execute manual and automated tests across multiple projects, often concurrently as required.
• Develop test planning and reporting deliverables.
• Participate in the review of requirements and specifications documentation.
• Conduct detailed analysis of system and business requirements, using these products to design test cases that will satisfy relevant objectives.
• Identify and support capability development and process improvement opportunities for the Testing services function in both manual and automation space.
• Contribute to knowledge transfer among team members.
• Develop and maintain productive working relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
• management of defects including reporting using tools such as JIRA
• Review test cases to ensure accuracy, completeness, traceability and coverage of business requirements and risks.
• Certification in ISTQB is highly desirable.
• Certification in Tricentis TOSCA desirable.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Invite only: ERP program manager

Tenders.Net #: 1602767
Agency: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport and the Arts
Location: ACT
Closing: 26/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06245 - 1 ERP Program Manager
Job details
The Enterprise Resourcing Planning Branch (ERPB) is seeking to engage Senior Program Manager to support the commencement of a large-scale enterprise transformation program implementing a new ERP platform across the organisation.
This role will play a critical part in driving and maintaining an integrated delivery schedule and ensuring alignment with program objectives, timelines and governance requirements.
The ideal candidate will have demonstrated experience in leading and managing large scale ERP implementation projects, leading cross functional teams, identifying and resolving impediments and be fully competent in delivering projects throughout the entire project lifecycle.
Key duties and responsibilities
The Program Manager will:
• lead and coordinate program delivery activities to support the achievement of the department’s desired outcomes.
• develop, maintain, monitor and report with program management artefacts such as a roadmap, integrated program management plan, status of the deliverables, milestones, dependencies, resources, budget, risks and issues.
• oversee day-to-day project activities, ensuring progress aligns with the project plan.
• engage and collaborate with business stakeholders and suppliers as required to deliver a well-defined and understood solution.
• Build strong relationships and partner with the Senior Responsible Officer (SRO), system integrator, change manager, technology, and senior executives within the Program Board.
• Report to and will support the SRO in delivering the ERP implementation project.
• be accountable for ensuring that the contract with the system integrator is executed in accordance with the terms and conditions agreed by the department, including scope, deliverables, performance obligations, and governance requirements.
• undertake detailed risk analysis and actively manage the program’s key risks and issues, including establishing mitigation strategies, contingency plans, ownership and due dates, conducting regular risk reviews, and escalating material risks to the ERP Program Board through established governance processes.
• manage the development of the required artefacts for the project in line with department’s governance and assurance frameworks.
• engage and collaborate with other project streams to manage interdependencies, cross share capability and capacity, and ensure delivery of value-add, integrated solution.
• take responsibility for project progress and use of resources and initiate corrective action where necessary, foreshadowing upcoming resource needs and actioning engagement well ahead of start dates.
• provide reports and attend ERP Program Board and Committee meetings (once formal governance structure is formed) with leadership.
• be the primary liaison to the SI, monitoring progress against contracted deliverables, supporting acceptance processes and escalating issues of performance or scope in accordance with the program’s governance and contracted framework.
• develop project closure artefacts including lessons learned and operational transition activities.
The specified personnel may be directed to assist with other work in relation to other streams within the ERP Branch.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: PCS-03866 - ERP - Program management services
Requirements
The Enterprise Resourcing Planning Branch (ERPB) is seeking to engage Program management services to support the commencement of a large-scale enterprise transformation program implementing a new ERP platform across the organisation.
This capability will play a critical part in driving and maintaining an integrated delivery schedule and ensuring alignment with program objectives, timelines and governance requirements.
The ideal candidate will have demonstrated experience in leading and managing large scale ERP implementation projects, leading cross functional teams, identifying and resolving impediments and be fully competent in delivering projects throughout the entire project lifecycle.
Duties include:
• lead and coordinate program delivery activities to support the achievement of the department’s desired outcomes.
• develop, maintain, monitor and report with program management artefacts such as a roadmap, integrated program management plan, status of the deliverables, milestones, dependencies, resources, budget, risks and issues.
• oversee day-to-day project activities, ensuring progress aligns with the project plan.
• engage and collaborate with business stakeholders and suppliers as required to deliver a well-defined and understood solution.
• Build strong relationships and partner with the Senior Responsible Officer (SRO), system integrator, change manager, technology, and senior executives within the Program Board.
• Report to and will support the SRO in delivering the ERP implementation project.
• be accountable for ensuring that the contract with the system integrator is executed in accordance with the terms and conditions agreed by the department, including scope, deliverables, performance obligations, and governance requirements.
• undertake detailed risk analysis and actively manage the program’s key risks and issues, including establishing mitigation strategies, contingency plans, ownership and due dates, conducting regular risk reviews, and escalating material risks to the ERP Program Board through established governance processes.
• manage the development of the required artefacts for the project in line with department’s governance and assurance frameworks.
• engage and collaborate with other project streams to manage interdependencies, cross share capability and capacity, and ensure delivery of value-add, integrated solution.
• take responsibility for project progress and use of resources and initiate corrective action where necessary, foreshadowing upcoming resource needs and actioning engagement well ahead of start dates.
• provide reports and attend ERP Program Board and Committee meetings (once formal governance structure is formed) with leadership.
• be the primary liaison to the SI, monitoring progress against contracted deliverables, supporting acceptance processes and escalating issues of performance or scope in accordance with the program’s governance and contracted framework.
• develop project closure artefacts including lessons learned and operational transition activities.
The specified personnel may be directed to assist with other work in relation to other streams within the ERP Branch.

Invite only: FinOps engineer

Tenders.Net #: 1602764
Agency: Australian Electoral Commission
Location: ACT
Closing: 27/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions
Friday, 24 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-05624 - 1 FinOps Engineer
Job details
The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) is seeking to engage an experienced FinOps Engineer whose primary focus is on improving cloud resource allocation and supporting business teams in optimising their cloud utilisation. The role will also provide technical direction and advice for tasks, processes, and procedures to support the implementation of FinOps best practices.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Cloud Cost Optimisation: specialising in managing cloud costs through monitoring for anomalies and responding to alerts of increases in costs. This includes undertaking research and working closely with cross-functional teams, providing recommendations to optimize cloud usage, reduce waste, and improve cost efficiencies.
• Cloud Usage Analysis: Analysing cloud usage patterns and identify cost optimization opportunities. This involves understanding resource consumption, identifying underutilized resources, and suggesting adjustments.
• Cost Allocation and Chargeback: the set up of cost allocation mechanisms to attribute cloud expenses to specific teams or projects. They also establish chargeback processes to allocate costs accurately.
• Collaboration: Collaborate closely with engineering, finance, and business teams. By fostering communication and collaboration, they ensure that cloud spending aligns with business goals.
• Data-Driven Decision Making: the setup of practices that enable timely and informed decisions by providing financial accountability through data-driven insights. They develop cloud spend and usage reports to help organizations make informed choices about cloud.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Invite only: Project managers

Tenders.Net #: 1602700
Agency: Department of the Treasury
Location: ACT
Closing: 26/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: LH-06249 - 4 Manager Project Managers
The Treasury is seeking experienced Project Managers to play a critical role in delivering ICT projects on time and within budget. Successful candidates will demonstrate strong experience in managing IT projects and driving business and digital process improvement initiatives, with the ability to apply industry-standard methodologies, tools, and frameworks.
The candidate will have the ability to quickly adapt to the Treasury environment by engaging with key business users and stakeholders from various levels. They will be comfortable working both collaboratively within a team and autonomously to achieve successful outcomes.
The role requires strong project management capability in complex delivery environments, including initiatives with multiple stakeholders, dependencies and external partners. Projects may span planning, design, delivery, transition and review phases, and require coordination across organisational boundaries.
We are seeking candidates with at least 5 years documented and proven experience to lead and deliver projects of this scale and scope in an outcomes focused environment for the Commonwealth or equivalent to the scale and scope of Department of Treasury.
The intent of this process is to hire multiple Project Managers immediately and/or include interviews and negotiations regarding start/end dates and hourly rates. Following this, Treasury may utilise the outcome of this approach to market for up to three months from the closing date to engage other project managers from various suppliers involved in the process, leaving the evaluation process open to allow this to be achieved. Due to the panel currently requiring all contracts to be signed prior to notifying unsuccessful suppliers, those invited should note until all contracts are signed the RFQ will remain in "In Negotiation" phase. Additionally, no further suppliers will be invited to the RFQ due to line area evaluation resources in the peak pre-budget timeframe.
Key duties and responsibilities
• Participate and assisting in high-level planning for program and project implementation
• Define and maintain project scope, ensuring alignment with organisational goals and objectives, and effectively manage scope changes as required.
• Develop and manage project plans, schedules, budgets, and resource allocations to ensure delivery within agreed timeframes and constraints.
• Lead and coordinate multi-functional project teams, fostering collaboration and accountability to achieve delivery outcomes.
• Build and maintain effective working relationships with technical resources and stakeholders.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Invite only: DevOps engineers

Tenders.Net #: 1602697
Agency: Australian Crime Commission
Location: ACT
Closing: 04/05/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

E: ICTSourcing@acic.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Wednesday, 29 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: LH-06250 - 2 DevOps Engineers
The Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission (ACIC) is seeking to engage a suitably qualified and experienced DevOps Engineer to:
• Assist the Infrastructure team of the NCIS Program.
• Design, implement and maintain the security of Azure cloud platform and services.
• Establish and maintain strong working relationships with internal and external parties, including senior stakeholders.
Share technical knowledge across the team, and provide support to other parts of the NCIS Program.
Key duties and responsibilities
Implementing technical solutions in cloud to facilitate migration of applications.
• Designing infrastructure solutions to host applications currently on premise.
• Developing automated solutions to deploy infrastructure and applications to cloud.
• Working within DevOps practices of the wider program team.
• Inputting features and design of cloud foundations.
• Developing new standard features and automation for standard customer cloud environments.
• Contributing to Product management practices to develop new features.
• Contributing to agile/scrum ceremonies to develop, plan, and prioritise new feature delivery.
• Supporting Java applications running in a Linux environment.
• Undertaking performance tuning.
• Working in a diverse team across a wide range of different technologies and systems.
• Automating processes and building self-service capabilities.
• Delivering and building services and technologies including but not limited to Microsoft Azure.
• Leading and coordinating cloud development activities between relevant stakeholders and technical resources - both internal and external.
• Periodically collaborating to develop and implement operational improvements.
• Leveraging experience with cloud native tools to review metrics and monitor the health/utilisation of agency’s cloud services.
• Designing, implementing and maintaining the security of Azure cloud platform and services.
• Implementing new security measures on the Azure cloud.
• Collaborating with developers and the NCIS Sustainment team to create strategies for improving security.
• Scripting and automating security processes using Microsoft Azure DevOps.
• Setting direction for use of Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC), Microsoft Defender for Cloud, Administrator protection strategy and Azure policy to govern Azure resources.
• Managing vulnerability scanning tools to identify security risk in software code or platform.
• Responding to security incidents if required.
• Working with architects, administrators, and developers to plan and implement solutions that meet security and compliance requirements.
• Ensuring that Azure deployments are secure and compliant with industry and regulatory standard, the primary work focus will be infrastructure work that improves NCIS security posture.
• Specified Personnel will be expected to transfer skills and knowledge to departmental staff and ensure appropriate documentation is stored in accordance with the Department’s recordkeeping policy and practices.
• All services and deliverables performed by specified personnel must:
1. be performed or delivered promptly and without delay in accordance with the timeframes notified by the buyer from time to time; and
2. meet the assessment and performance criteria set out in any monitoring and performance assessment processes notified to the seller from time to time.
The ACIC requires specified personnel to:
Obtain the ACIC’s Organisational Suitability Assessment (OSA) prior to commencement.
• Have an Australian Government security clearance (AGSVA) to the minimum of Negative Vetting Level 1 (NV1) or be willing to undergo and obtain and AGSVA NV1 clearance.
• As directed by the ACIC, complete a Deed of Confidentiality or Non-Disclosure Agreement prior to commencement or comply with processes required to engage the Specified Personnel under section 48 of the Australian Crime Commission Act (2002).
Note: Seller may be required to meet the cost of any security clearances

Invite only: ERP - organisational change management services

Tenders.Net #: 1602694
Agency: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport and the Arts
Location: ACT
Closing: 26/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Thursday, 23 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: PCS-03858 - ERP - Organisational Change management services
The department is looking for an experienced organisational change management (OCM) capability to support the commencement of a large-scale enterprise transformation program implementing a new ERP platform across the organisation.
This OCM capability will play a critical part in the ERP Program driving business readiness, stakeholder engagement, and adoption across a complex, multi-vendor environment. The OCM capability should demonstrate proven ERP expertise to provide advice and direction on the current ERP Program change strategy.
The OCM capability will:
• review existing and / or develop change management artefacts (i.e. strategy, plan, change impact assessments), to address diverse stakeholder groups and alignment with the new ERP platform
• develop and support the development of new change management resources, tools and templates
• support the Buyer’s leadership team in driving change messaging and reinforcing behavioural shifts
• oversee stakeholder engagement, targeted communications and adoption planning tailored to ERP Program’s needs
• be supported by in-house change management practitioner, ERP Program support officers and a communications team fostering a collaborative approach
• progress the ERP change management model and key change priorities, and
• work collaboratively with suppliers, vendors, business units and delivery teams.
The specified personnel may be directed to assist with other work in relation to other streams within the ERP Branch.
If the department elects to extend the contract past the initial term, the ERP Program will facilitate significant system and process change and will require strong OCM experience within ERP environments. During the optional term(s) of the Contract the OCM capability will:
• lead the associated change activities during the implementation of a new ERP Platform across the organisation (i.e. engagement strategies)
• work closely with ERP Program leadership, business SMEs, technology teams and the ERP platform implementation partner to ensure change impacts are understood, managed and embedded effectively
• lead stakeholder engagement, business readiness assessments / metrics and adoption activities
• partner with training teams to ensure effective learning and capability uplift
• develop and track a benefits realisation plan and report these to the PB, identifying if any benefit is at risk, so that scope or change management interventions can be made.
• monitor change risks, resistance and adoption metrics across the ERP Program, and
• integrate change management practices throughout the entire program lifecycle to drive both people and process transformation in parallel with technology implementation.

Tenders.Net #: 1602686
Agency: Services Australia
Location: ACT
Closing: 30/04/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Tuesday, 28 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: PCS-03855 - MST26-011 Agentic AI Strategy Analysis
This procurement is intended to address a capability and capacity gap in the agency’s ability to independently assess and design for agentic AI. The agency requires a Seller to provide expert, vendor-agnostic analysis of available approaches and to test feasibility and implications for the agency’s environment.
The Seller will also support the agency to uplift internal capability through practical guidance, training and reusable resources, and ensure recommendations align with agency and whole-of-government frameworks (including for system design, risk assessment, assurance and implementation planning).
The ideal end state is that the agency has a clear, evidence based and defensible position on where agentic AI could be used, an agreed readiness and gap assessment, and a roadmap and capability model that enables the agency to make informed investment and governance decisions and progress priority use cases in a controlled, responsible and repeatable way.

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*

Digital Transformation Agency - Buy ICT
P: 02 6120 8705
E: ictprocurement@dta.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Thursday, 30 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time
Details: PCS-03854 - ICT Professional services to support ICT Delivery and Enablement Projects 2026
The purpose of this procurement is to establish an ICT Professional services Panel to provide the Department of Education (the department) with flexible, ondemand access to Professional and Consulting services to support the delivery of a range of ICT and digital initiatives and projects of varying size, complexity and duration across the department.
The department is seeking to establish a panel by engaging sellers (up to 5) for 24 months (with an up to 1 x 12-month extension if required) to provide ICT Professional and Consultancy services across various disciplines, on an as needed basis.
Successful sellers must be flexible and able to work closely with business areas to respond to changing requirements. This includes being highly responsive to requests, providing timely advice, and delivering a positive customer experience for business stakeholders. Sellers are expected to operate collaboratively and maintain clear, professional communication throughout engagements.
Sellers are expected to demonstrate a strong commitment to the department and to delivering high quality, collaborative engagement experiences that support timely, effective, and value for money outcomes.
Sellers must have the capability and experience to maintain, source, and mobilise suitably qualified and experienced personnel at short notice when requested by business areas. This includes the ability to support multiple concurrent engagements across different business areas, where required, and to ensure continuity of service across Work Orders. This approach will enable the department to onboard ICT professional and consultancy resources efficiently and deliver outcomes in a timely manner.
Sellers must ensure that any specialists or experts proposed to the department hold current police checks and minimum baseline security clearances, to support efficient onboarding and minimise delays to commencement.
Successful suppliers may be required to deliver one or more of the following deliverables, as specified in individual Work Orders. The nature, scope, and timing of deliverables will vary depending on business requirements, the needs of business owners, the CIO function, and the stage and progress of each project.
Deliverables and capability may include, but are not limited to:
• ICT advisory services, including the provision of expert strategic, technical, operational, architectural, assurance, governance, security, compliance, or service management advice and recommendations
• Business and technical analysis, including requirements definition, documentation, and stakeholder engagement
• Development of plans, reports, strategies, design artefacts, and other agreed documentation
• Delivery of agreed outcomes, milestones, or work packages
• ICT project and program support, including planning, coordination, reporting, risk and issue management, and stakeholder engagement
• ICT solution design, implementation support, testing, deployment, transition, and handover activities
• Operational and postdeployment support activities, where required
• Knowledge transfer to departmental staff, including handover documentation, where appropriate, and
• Support for the continuous improvement of ICT practices, processes, or systems.
All deliverables must be fit for purpose, meet agreed quality and performance standards, and be delivered in accordance with the relevant Work Order. Deliverables will be subject to review and formal acceptance by the Department.
The contract put in place with the successful seller/s will be of a value anticipated to be the maximum value estimated for ICT Professional services at the time the procurement is finalised. The Department is unable to make any guarantees on the number of ICT Professional services sellers eventually engaged and or periods of engagement.

Agency: eSafety Commissioner
Location: ACT
Closing: 04/05/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

E: ICTProcurements@esafety.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Monday, 27 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: PCS-03860 - Totara LMS Managed services
The Australian Government, through the Office of the eSafety Commissioner (eSafety), is seeking to procure a suitably qualified supplier via the Digital Marketplace to deliver ongoing managed services for the Be Connected (https://beconnected.esafety.gov.au/) and Frontline Worker’s (https://frontlineworkers.esafety.gov.au/) websites.
Be Connected is a national digital inclusion initiative designed to improve the confidence, digital skills and online safety of older Australians, supported through online learning resources and a network of community organisations.
The Frontline Workers website provides online training for frontline workers, delivering in-depth training to enable them to better help their clients experiencing technology-facilitated abuse.
Both websites are built on the Totara Learn LMS platform and collectively provide access to hundreds of learning modules, courses, and resources for their respective cohorts.

Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

E: ICTProcurements@esafety.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Monday, 27 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: PCS-03859 - security services
The eSafety Commissioner’s (eSafety) purpose is to help safeguard Australians at risk from online harms and to promote safer, more positive online experiences.
eSafety leads and coordinates the online safety efforts across Australian Government departments, authorities and agencies, and engages with key online safety stakeholders. It has a broad remit which includes administering complaint schemes and the development of audience-specific content and programs to help educate all Australians about online safety risks and how to deal with harms including cyberbullying, adult cyber abuse, image-based abuse and illegal and restricted content.
This RFQ seeks to engage a qualified security services provider to deliver penetration testing and PSPF/ISM-aligned security assessments of IT Systems.
Due to variability in system size, complexity, and scope, services will be procured on a fee-for-service basis, with work commissioned via individual work orders.
The contract will include a Do Not Exceed (DNE) maximum value, with services delivered as required over the contract term, and agreed upon before commencement.

Invite only: Service desk analyst

Tenders.Net #: 1602693
Agency: Australian Transaction Reports & Analysis Centre (AUSTRAC)
Location: NSW
Closing: 01/05/2026 - 11:59 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Invited Sellers Only.

E: procurement@austrac.gov.au

Deadline for asking questions Wednesday, 29 April 2026 • 11:59pm, Canberra time.
Details: LH-06238 - 1 Service Desk Analyst
AUSTRAC is seeking a full-time onsite Service Desk Analyst to support end users of IT systems and services within the Information and Technology Solutions (ITS) Branch.
The Service Desk Analyst will act as a key frontline support resource in the Sydney office, providing high-quality onsite and remote assistance, managing incidents and service requests, provisioning and supporting end-user equipment, and contributing to the effective operation of AUSTRAC’s IT support environment.
The successful candidate will possess skills and experience aligned to a Service Desk Analyst role at the APS5 equivalent level. They will otherwise demonstrate the skills and experience under "Key duties and responsibilities" below.
Key duties and responsibilities
The Service Desk Analyst is a key member of the IT Service Desk within the Information & Technology Solutions (ITS) Branch.
The IT Service Desk works collaboratively to provide clear, effective and responsive IT support, ensuring the staff of AUSTRAC can use agency technology with confidence.
The Service Desk Analyst in each state office is responsible for day-to-day operational support. They must have the ability to work under limited supervision in resolving a wide range of technical issues while also providing high-quality user experience.
The key duties and requirements for the role are as provided below.
Key Duties
The Service Desk Analyst is responsible for:
• Providing high-quality onsite and remote IT support, ensuring timely resolution of tickets for all staff, including support for Senior Executives.
• Managing and actioning service requests and incidents in alignment with established ITIL processes, maintaining clear communication with stakeholders and delivering excellent customer service.
• Administering core systems, user accounts, permissions, and network resources in accordance with organisational policies and security requirements.
• Working collaboratively with team members, platform teams, and external vendors to support the delivery of IT services.
• Managing end-user IT equipment and peripherals, including troubleshooting and resolving hardware/software incidents, assisting with deployment of new or replacement devices, and maintaining accurate asset records.
• Sharing technical expertise and contributing to a knowledge-sharing team culture.
• Participating in the testing and implementation of new services, tools, and processes that enhance IT operations.
• Creating and maintaining clear technical documentation and user guides to support IT processes and assist staff.
• Ensuring compliance with corporate security policies and proactively identify and report risks in the IT environment.
• Undertaking other duties as directed, aligned with the priorities of the ITS branch.
Requirements for the Role
The Service Desk Analyst will possess the Core capabilities and Professional capabilities outlined below.
Core capabilities
Deliver Client Focussed Results
Takes personal responsibility for service provided to clients. Ensures a high level of responsiveness and accessibility to clients. Works to strengthen and maintain relations with clients from culturally and linguistically diverse groups. Monitors and manages client service delivery and ensures that it is accurate, timely, consistent and appropriate. Seeks feedback from clients on satisfaction and areas for potential improvement.
Apply Expertise
Understands the technical and professional skills and expertise in the work area. Ensures effective use of own and team’s expertise in supporting work area objectives. Consults appropriately and includes others to ensure comprehensive professional input is used. Provides appropriate advice in area of professional or technical specialisation. Resolves problems effectively using established practices and methodologies relevant to area of specialisation. Ensures knowledge, skills and expertise are current and appropriate to complete work objectives.
Communicate Effectively
Communicates clearly, concisely and unambiguously. Acts as an effective representative of work area. Engages in active listening and seeks clarification of own understanding. Tailors communication to audience and circumstances and ensures common understanding of issues. Encourages discussion, welcomes and responds to feedback and adapts approach to ensure audience understanding.
Adapt and Respond to Change
Participates in the implementation of change initiatives. Accommodates changing priorities and operating environments and responds with initiative and flexibility to periods of uncertainty and ambiguity. Modifies own work practices to support changing circumstances and organisational focus. Actively helps and supports others in dealing with uncertainty and change.
People Focussed
Works collaboratively with team members and with a range of internal and external clients. Expresses own views in a constructive and diplomatic way. Recognises contributions made by others and rewards achievements. Reflects on how own emotions impact on others.
Values Based
Demonstrates high levels of professionalism and independence. Provides a high level of honest and impartial advice. Provides leadership in ethical behaviour. Promotes and upholds relevant codes of ethics and practice and aligns business processes accordingly. Accepts responsibility for and acknowledges mistakes and is willing to seek advice from others. displays the courage and commitment to maintain a difficult position. Is prepared to address difficult issues. Recognises individual differences and working styles and manages diversity in the workplace.
Professional capabilities
Technical Proficiency
Demonstrated technical expertise solving problems in a modern Microsoft end-user environment, some examples include:
• Windows 11
• Microsoft Intune
• Active Directory and Azure Active Directory (Entra ID)
• Hardware including Microsoft Surface Pro, Apple iPhones, Cisco Videoconferencing and MFDs
• Microsoft 365, Office Apps and Exchange online.
• Basic Network Troubleshooting (VPN, LAN)
• ITSM tools (e.g. Jira, ServiceNow)
Operates in alignment with service management principles and best‑practice frameworks (e.g., ITIL v4), applying these to ensure consistent IT service delivery.
Location of services: Sydney, NSW
Clearance required: Ability to obtain and maintain Negative Vetting 1 (NV1), with an existing NV1 clearance being desirable
Suitability assessment: Labour Hire Workers must pass an Agency Suitability Assessment before being able to commence with AUSTRAC

Details: WS2068596207 - Waste management Infrastructure
Supply, delivery and installation 60L bins (general waste, comingled recycling, paper and cardboard recycling) to create centralised and standardised waste collection stations across campus portfolio.
Project goals
TAFE NSW are taking a two stage process to engage an appropriate supplier.
Stage 1 – Registration for Opportunity - see attachment
Stage 2 – TAFE NSW will issue an invitation to the RFP via TAFE NSW Ariba
Scope
At a minimum, the project needs to address the requirements of the Core services shown in the attached Summary Scope of services. The core services cover a minimum of three waste source streams (general waste, commingled recycling, paper and cardboard recycling).
The supplier is expected to support TAFE NSW to:
· Confirm optimal locations and bin combinations to maximise landfill diversion
· Replace ad hoc bins with centralised waste stations in common areas
· Supply, deliver, and install 60L bins and associated signage
· Enable TAFE NSW to custom design signage to suit operational needs
· Provide education, training, and change‑management resources
· Develop a case study measuring environmental, financial, and behavioural outcomes
Consistency, safety, and alignment with TAFE NSW standards are critical throughout the roll‑out.

Advertising billboards - operate, manage & maintain

Tenders.Net #: 1602660
Agency: Transport for NSW
Location: NSW
Closing: 29/05/2026 - 03:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: David Hanna
E: david.hanna@transport.nsw.gov.au
P: 0448 301 602
Details: WS5622271244 - Existing advertising billboards
TfNSW is looking to partner with out-of-home (OOH) supplier/s to operate, manage and maintain 62 static and 34 digital OOH advertising roadside assets located in metro and regional NSW.
Project goals
Proposals from Respondents to undertake the development, management and operation of Existing Advertising assets (static and digital billboard assets) including the upgrade of static assets to digital and the decommissioning of static assets within this RFP.
The proposed contract terms are:
• Digital billboards – 5 years, plus a single, 2-year option
• Static billboards – 3 years, plus a single, 2-year option
• Static billboards upgraded to digital – 7 years, plus a single, 2-year option
Scope
The Supplier must work collaboratively with TfNSW to achieve the following key objectives:
• meet and exceed the outcomes, obligations and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the Proposed Contract;
• efficiently plan and obtain DA consents from the Department of Planning and Environment (DPE) to upgrade static assets to digital;
• build state-of-the-art digital Advertising assets in a timely, efficient, and safe manner;
• position the in-scope Advertising assets as a premium asset within the OOH marketplace;
• provide a strong commercial revenue stream to TfNSW that exceeds annual sales targets and grows year on year;
• minimise exposure to various risks by adhering to relevant standards, policies and legislation (safety, development protocols, planning, advertising content standards etc)
• innovate to ensure the increasing sustainability of advertising revenue generated;
• report performance metrics to TfNSW; and
• propose new advertising strategies to TfNSW that are consistent with the requirements of TfNSW.

Details: SR01142 - Customer contact answering service for NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service
The NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service (NPWS), Policy and Engagement Branch, seeks to engage an onshore single service provider for 24/7 call centre (answering) services.
Project goals
To provide a reliable, Australian-based 24/7 answering service that triages and resolves public and stakeholder calls for NPWS, escalates time‑critical matters appropriately, and conducts welfare checks to ensure the safety of lone and vulnerable NPWS staff across NSW.
Scope
Provision of an Australian‑based, onshore 24/7 answering service for NPWS to triage, resolve and escalate incoming public and stakeholder calls. The service also includes conducting lone worker welfare checks, completing after‑call actions, and meeting agreed technology, security and performance requirements.

Tenders.Net #: 1602601
Agency: Department of Communities and Justice
Location: NSW
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: CBP Team
Department of Communities and Justice
P: (02) 8753 8144
E: CBP2026@dcj.nsw.gov.au
Details: Community Building Partnership 2026
The Community Building Partnership (CBP) program provides grant funding to local community organisations and councils to strengthen the wellbeing of NSW communities through small local infrastructure projects.
Grant amount: From $10,000 to $100,000
The Community Building Partnership (CBP) program provides grant funding to local community organisations and councils to strengthen the wellbeing of NSW communities through small local infrastructure projects.
• Total funding available for the CBP round in 2026 in $41.85 million.
• There is a maximum of $450,000 grant funding available in each electoral district.
Who can apply
To be eligible to apply for funding, applicants must be one of the following organisation types:
• incorporated association registered with NSW Fair Trading
• Australian public company limited by guarantee
• Local Aboriginal Land Council
• Indigenous corporation registered with the Office of the Registrar of Indigenous Corporations
• NSW local council
• council committee under section 355 of the Local Government Act 1993
• trust registered with the Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission (ACNC)
• P&C Association registered by NSW Fair Trading
• P&C Association registered by the Federation of Parents and Citizens Associations of NSW
• not-for-profit entity incorporated under an Act of Parliament
• non-distributing co-operative registered with NSW Fair Trading
Eligible applicants must also hold a valid and current Certificate of Currency for Public Liability Insurance with a minimum cover of $5 million.
Types of projects funded under this grant
CBP will consider applications between $10,000 and $100,000 for 3 categories:
1. Capital works
Capital works are defined as ‘improvements to the land or building, or freestanding equipment that will be fixed or installed to the land or building’. This includes:
• construction of new community infrastructure.
• upgrade (refurbishment, repair and maintenance) to existing community infrastructure.
• any project component that requires fixed installation and would require permission from the owner of the building/land to commence works. This applies even if your organisation is the owner and/or other project components are freestanding equipment.
2. Equipment
Equipment is defined as ‘freestanding equipment that does not require fixed installation and/or registration’. This includes:
• vehicles that do not require registration, e.g. tractors, utility task vehicles (UTVs), all-terrain vehicles (ATVs),dragon boats, golf buggies, dinghies, powerboats or sailing boats less than 5.5 m long or with an engineless than 4.0 kW.
• Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs). Applications are limited to one package up to $3,000 per application, per project location, and will be deemed ineligible if it is for more than one AED package at one project location.
3. Vehicles
Vehicles are defined as ‘vehicles that require registration’. This includes:
• purchase or modification of vehicles that require registration.
• on-road vehicles such as cars, buses, trucks, and trailers.
• off-road or on-water vehicles such as motorised rescue vehicles and boats.
All projects must be delivered in NSW within the Electoral District for which funding is sought from.
When the project can start and end
The project should be started by 8 May 2026 and the project must be completed by 24 January 2028.
What co-contributions are required
Local councils and council committees under section 355 of the Local Government Act 1993 are required to provide a matched cash contribution equal to the grant amount requested.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Creative director (Carols by Candlelight)

Tenders.Net #: 1602544
Agency: Griffith City Council
Location: NSW
Closing: 29/05/2026 - COB
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Submissions and questions can be sent to:
Viviana Degenkamp
E: ed@griffith.nsw.gov.au
Details: EOI - Creative Director for Griffith Carols by Candlelight 2026
Griffith City Council is seeking a Creative Director to coordinate and deliver the Griffith Carols by Candlelight on Sunday 13 December 2026 at the Griffith Community Stage, Community Gardens.
The successful applicant will be responsible for ensuring that the event is developed, designed and delivered on time and on budget, to a high standard. They will work closely with Council Event management Staff to ensure a quality program is achieved. Tasks will include:
• Creating a suitable Carols by Candlelight program
• Engage a production team to deliver the event
• Coordinate musicians, singers and other performers
• Any other associated tasks.
The Creative Director will be paid a total of $5,000 for the successful delivery of the event.
EOI Submissions should contain:
• Your understanding and vision for this event
• A brief outline of your proposal to conduct the event
• Any proposed key personnel and timeline
• Your confirmed availability from July - December 2026
• Details of relevant experience.

Details: TSC 03/2026 - Supply and Installation of Solar PV and Battery Storage Systems
Supply and Installation of Solar PV and Battery Storage Systems - Temora, NSW 2666
Temora Shire Council is a Local Government Organisation in the Riverina region of New South Wales (2666) and is situated within the Southwest Slopes area of Wiradjuri Council approximately 80km from Wagga Wagga, 150km from Canberra and 450km from Sydney.
Temora Shire Council (the Principal) is seeking to install solar photovoltaic (PV) and Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) at two of its operational facilities. These installations form part of Council’s commitment to reducing energy costs and improving energy resilience across its infrastructure portfolio. Site 1 is Temora Recreation Center with a 49.5kW Solar and 80kWh battery. Site 2 is Temora Sewage Treatment Plant with a 39.9kW solar and 78kWh battery. A ley requirement of this project is the operation of both battery and solar systems as part of a Virtual Power Plant (VPP).
Temora Shire Council is aiming for the system to be operational for Site 1 by 30 June 2026 and for Site 2 by 21 December 2026.

Details: 26.0000129150.0103 - HW2 Partridge VC Rest Area - Amenities Building Upgrade
Tenderers are invited for HW2 Partridge VC Rest Area - Amenities Building Upgrade. Works are to be performed in accordance with project designs, reports and environmental requirements.
This work consists of design, supply and installation of public amenities (restrooms), concreting including demolition of the existing amenities building.
The scope of work generally comprises the following:
• Demolition of the existing amenities building at Partridge VC Rest Area and disposal of building waste.
• Detailed design of a replacement amenities building, consisting of 6 ambulant and 2 accessible access toilets. A concept design is provided in Section 2.
• Design and construction of a concrete slab at the existing amenities building location and connection to existing sewer system and tank water supply.
• Fabrication of the amenities building.
• Delivery and installation of amenities building at Partridge VC Rest Area.

Climate-related financial disclosures

Tenders.Net #: 1602785
Agency: CQUniversity Australia
Location: QLD
Closing: 20/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Central Queensland University
Procurement Advice Team
E: pat-requests@cqu.edu.au
Details: RFP Climate-Related Financial Disclosures
Central Queensland University (CQU) is seeking Proposals from suitably qualified and experienced consultants to design and propose a modular, scalable approach to preparing for emerging climate-related financial disclosure requirements, noting these are not yet mandated for statutory bodies in Queensland.
This invitation deliberately does not prescribe a fixed scope of works. Suppliers are asked to apply their expertise to:
a) articulate what services are required to support compliance with, and readiness for, climate-related financial disclosure frameworks such as Australian Accounting Standards Board AASB S2, International Sustainability Standards, the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures framework and broader Australian public sector and higher education regulatory expectations including NSW Treasury’s TPG 24-33,
b) distinguish between core foundational services, optional or advanced services, and future-phase services as disclosure maturity evolves, and
c) propose a delivery model that supports both institution-specific requirements and shared, multi-institution solutions that would be applicable across regional universities.
In addition to CQU’s own needs, this Invitation expressly seeks proposals that identify opportunities for collaboration across Reginal Universities Network (RUN) institutions, including:
• services and deliverables that can be standardised or reused across multiple universities,
• shared tools, frameworks, template or guidance materials,
• joint training or delivery models,
• pricing structures that demonstrate economies of scale where multiple institutions participate.
While CQU will contract in its own right, suppliers are asked to proactively identify, design and clearly articulate where shared RUN-level solutions are appropriate and how these could be practically implemented.

Construction of water treatment plant upgrade

Tenders.Net #: 1602689
Agency: Department of Local Government, Water and Volunteers
Location: QLD
Closing: 28/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: Procurement@rdmw.qld.gov.au

Please note that a site visit and an industry briefing will be held as follows:
Site Visit - 5 May 2026 at 10 am
Industry Briefing - 7 May 2026 at 10 am

Tenderers are required to register their attendance by sending an email to Procurement@dlgwv.qld.gov.au to the attention of Florine Vega-Hughes, detailing the name/s and contact details of attendees (email address and mobile number). Please refer to the Invitation to Offer document for further details.
Details: DLGWV2025-0153 - Construction of the Cherbourg water Treatment Plant Upgrade
The Department of Local Government, water and Volunteers is seeking to engage a contractor to undertake the Construction of the Cherbourg water Treatment Plant Upgrade to address long-standing challenges with drinking water quality in the community. These challenges include persistent Boil water Advisories (BWAs), low chlorine residuals, E.coli detections, and elevated disinfection by-products, primarily caused by ageing infrastructure, limited instrumentation, and inadequate treatment processes at the existing facility. The project aims to implement critical improvements to eliminate these risks and ensure the community has access to a safe, reliable, and sustainable drinking water supply.
Cherbourg is an Aboriginal community of the Wakka Wakka people, located 267 km northwest of Brisbane within the South Burnett Region. The community has an estimated population of 1,190 residents according to the 2021 census, though Cherbourg Aboriginal Shire Council (CASC) suggests the actual population is closer to 1,800. With project population growth to 4,000 by 2070, the demand for essential services including safe and reliable drinking water is expected to increase significantly.
Cherbourg’s water supply is sourced from “The Rocks” in Barambah Creek. The Bjelke-Petersen Dam, managed by Sunwater, can augment the Barambah Creek supply through releases, though this is rare. water is pumped from the creek to a conventional water treatment plant that consists of a clarifier followed by sand and carbon filters, before disinfection with chlorine gas. water is then stored in an arrangement (based on need and circumstance) across 3 service reservoirs prior to distribution to the supply network.
The WTP was established in 1970 and has undergone several upgrades over time, with several upgrades taking place between 2016 and 2022, including the installation of the 2x2ML reservoirs, addition of granulated activated carbon (GAC) filtration, and installing a new raw water rising main. Despite these upgrades, the WTP is significantly challenged in producing water that is compliant with desired water quality criteria. Data and information are also scarce and difficult to source, with immature asset management and no structured data/information historian, which limits the understanding and visibility of what is occurring.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: Advanced Tender Notice - Aerodrome Operations services - Redcliffe Aerodrome YRDE
The City of Moreton Bay (Council) provides Prospective Tenderers with an Advanced Tender Notice on its intent to invite Tenders for Aerodrome Operations services – Redcliffe Aerodrome (YRED).
BACKGROUND
City of Moreton Bay (Council) is the owner and operator of the Redcliffe Aerodrome (YRED) located on Wirraway Drive, Rothwell. The Redcliffe Aerodrome is used by recreational and commercial users, which includes flight training schools, small chartered flights, aircraft manufacturing and maintenance business, as well as community groups.
Council is seeking the services of an experienced aerodrome management provider for the delivery of aerodrome operations services through the provision of a suitably qualified Aerodrome Reporting Officer (ARO), operational management, facility management, ensure compliance to relevant safety standards, quality assurance protocols and ensuring industry best practice is met.
Council views this collaboration as essential to achieving operational efficiency and advancing Council’s broader objectives.
TENDER DETAILS
Council anticipates the opportunity to Tender will be invited via a Request for Tender through a separate event in May 2026.

Details: EOI 001/26 - Disposal of Work Camp Buildings
Pursuant to section 228(5) of the Local Government Regulation 2012, Aurukun Shire Council is calling for expressions of interest (“EOI”) for the disposal of several buildings from the site known as Airport Camp.
Respondents may submit an EOI for part of or all of the asset list, and must specify which buildings they intend to remove in their submission.
The buildings include:
Item - Description - Length (m) - Width (m)
1 - 5-bedroom donga - 12m- 3m
2 - 5-bedroom donga - 12m - 3m
3 - 5-bedroom donga - 12m - 3m
4 - 5-bedroom donga - 12m - 3m
5 - 5-bedroom donga - 12m - 3m
6 - Small kitchen - 2.8m - 3m
7 - 2 toilets/2 showers - 2.8m - 3m
8 - 2 toilets/2 showers - 6m - 3m
9 - 3 toilets/2 showers in shipping container - 6m - 3m
10 - Kitchen/dining room - 12m - 3m
11 - Storage cubicle - 2.5m - 2.5m
12 - Laundry - 5m - 1.5m
1. Aurukun Shire Council will arrange:
• Disconnection of electricity from main supply and make safe;
• Disconnection of water from main supply and make safe;
• Disconnection from sewerage and/or septic tank;
• Pumping out of septic tank; and
• Lifting and placement of assets on truck if required.
2. Successful respondent will arrange:
• Confirm when and where assets will be removed to – this is not to be in the Aurukun Local Government Area;
• Provide 14 days’ notice as to when assets will be removed;
• Provide suitable, safe and secure transport for assets;
• Comply with all applicable laws and regulations;
• Leave Airport Camp site in tidy condition; and
• Remove assets no later than 26 June 2026.

Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: If you have any questions or require further information, please contact Norman Donato, Partner at (02) 8035 7971 and email ndonato@millsoakley.com.au

Interested parties should register their interest before 30 April 2026. Upon entering a confidentiality agreement, they will be provided with an information memorandum and details of the sale process. Non-binding offers are to be submitted before 22 May 2026.
Details: Aurora Metals Limited
(Liquidators Appointed)
Mt Garnet Mineral Finance Pty Ltd (MGMF), as mortgagee in possession, invites expressions of interest for the purchase of the business facilities and assets of Aurora Metals Limited (in liquidation) and its subsidiaries (Group) in North Queensland.
Summary of Offering
• 6 mining leases; 14 exploration permits and 1 further mining lease application;
• 2 real property titles
• Portfolio of regionally focused assets, currently under care and maintenance, meeting Qld government requirements and authorities, comprising of:
Einsaleigh assets
- Einsaleigh Cooper: 4% Cu;
- Chloe and Jackson: polymetallic; and
- Real properties and plant and equipment
Other assets
- Tin assets: Lynd and Tate river;
Mills Oakley has been retained as legal adviser to MGMF to manage the sale process.

Personal protective equipment, PPE vending machine hire & services

Tenders.Net #: 1602581
Agency: Mackay Regional Council
Location: QLD
Closing: 11/05/2026 - 10:00 am
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Contract Team
E: contracts@mackay.qld.gov.au
Details: MRC-2026-PRSA-000042 - Supply and Delivery of Personal Protective Equipment, PPE Vending Machine Hire and services
Mackay Regional Council (Council) invites tenders from suitably qualified Tenderers for appointment as pre-qualified Supplier(s) for the reliable and continuous supply and delivery of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE). Council also seeks Tenderers with the capability to provide PPE vending machine hire and associated services, which will be considered as an optional component of this Request for Tender (RFT). The Scope of the required Goods and services is described in more detail in Part 5 – Scope.
The objective is to establish a contractual arrangement with Supplier(s) who can consistently deliver high-quality products across a broad range of categories. Successful tenderers will demonstrate the capability to meet Council’s requirements in relation to product range, quality, compliance with relevant standards, value for money, and reliable and timely delivery.
This RFT encompasses, but is not limited to, the supply of the following categories:
(a) General PPE products
(b) First-Aid Supplies
(c) Safety Boots
(d) (Optional) PPE Vending Machine Hire and services, including operation, restocking and maintenance.
Council may, at its discretion, make an award:
(a) award one or more categories to a single Tenderer;
(b) award different categories to different Tenderers;
(c) award individual goods, services, product groups or pricing schedule items within a category to different Tenderers; or
(d) elect not to award any category, item or component.

The Department of Education (DoE) is calling for submissions for the design and construction of a new Sports Training Precinct located at Benley Park College, located at McLaughlin Rd, Bentley Park, QLD, 4869. The works include, but are not limited to:
Sports Training Precinct

- One gym
- One kinesiology
- Canteen
- Meeting room
- Clean & store
- Amenities for both male and female students, including a PWD
- Data Room (ICT)
- Awning/undercover outdoor area
- Covered link to school
Tender submissions will be accepted from eligible contractors that satisfy the PQC requirements of this project.

Transmission lines & infrastructure (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1505908
Agency: Powerlink Transmission Services Pty Ltd
Location: QLD
Closing: 12/02/2027
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Please confirm details & closing date of this opportunity with contact.
NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 30/04/2025, 11/05/2025 & 20/02/2026. Please confirm with contact.
NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 20/04/2026.

Nimmi Pushparajan
P: +61 7 3364 0670
E: nimmi.pushparajan@icnqld.org.au
Details: The Gladstone Project
Overview
Powerlink is delivering the Gladstone Project which will strengthen and increase capacity of the electricity transmission network in the region. This critical program of transmission upgrades will ensure the safety and reliability of the power network as the region prepares for the eventual retirement of the Gladstone Power Station, and heavy industry moves toward using renewable energy sources.
The Gladstone Project will see several key projects planned, assessed and delivered in incremental stages over the next decade. This will include new transmission lines and new transmission infrastructure such as substations and associated equipment.
Stage 1
This first stage involves construction of the Calvale to Calliope River Transmission Line Reinforcement Project. Early works (planning and assessment) funding has been approved and is underway, and construction is expected to start by mid-2026. Completion is scheduled for late 2028.
Stage 1 will also include works at the existing Calvale Substation, and an expansion of the existing Calliope River Substation.
For further information on the planning and engagement activities completed to date, as well as the status of approvals, please refer to Powerlink's website: Calvale to Calliope River Transmission Line Reinforcement Project | Powerlink.
Future stages
Investment and funding decisions for future stages of the Gladstone Project will be assessed and delivered as part of the Queensland Government's Priority Transmission Investment (PTI) process. For further information on this investment process, please refer to Powerlink's website: Priority Transmission Investments | Powerlink.
The Gladstone Project is expected to be implemented in three stages, subject to several factors, including: the PTI assessment, Ministerial direction, responsiveness to the load growth in the region, and the need to be flexible and maximise delivery efficiency.
===

Agency: City of Launceston
Location: TAS

Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: NOTE: Further details via the link provided, pending registration to the principal's tendering portal.

Details Emma van Lint, Urban Forester, emma.vanlint@launceston.tas.gov.au.
Details: CD076/2026 (CD076/2025). As part of the City Heart Place Plan, the city seeks to appoint a suitably qualified, multi-disciplinary design team to deliver concept design, master planning and detailed design services for the greening and streetscape upgrade of Cameron St, Launceston CBD.
The project will investigate opportunities to establish a continuous tree avenue, improve pedestrian amenity and walkability, and integrate cycleway, traffic and public realm upgrades. The design will also consider activation opportunities, including the ability to accommodate markets and events, while responding to the street's heritage context and role as key east-west connection linking Royal Park, Civic Square and City Park.

Art submissions

Tenders.Net #: 1602783
Agency: Corangamite Catchment Management Authority
Location: VIC
Closing: 30/06/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Send submissions to: justine.holmes@ccma.vic.gov.au

To express interest or submit artwork, please provide:
Your name and connection to EstuaryWatch
Title of the work
Medium and approximate dimensions
A short description or story behind the piece (optional but encouraged)
Photos of the artwork (for 2D works or sculptures)
Details: Watching Over Estuaries: Call for Art Submissions
In 2026, EstuaryWatch celebrates 20 years of monitoring, learning, caring, and building connections with Victorian estuaries, thanks to the passion of volunteers, coordinators, partners, and communities across the state. To mark this special milestone, we are planning a 20‑year celebration event in late winter / early spring (date to be confirmed), and we invite you to be part of it by contributing to a community art exhibition (and also coming along to celebrate).
For 20 years, EstuaryWatch has been more than a program, it’s been a community of people who care deeply about estuaries and each other. This exhibition is a way to honour that shared journey, reflect on what we’ve learned, and celebrate the many ways people connect to water.
We warmly invite volunteers (past and present), staff, partners, and supporters to submit artworks inspired by EstuaryWatch and 20 years of estuary stories. These works will be exhibited as part of the EstuaryWatch 20-year celebration event (details still to be confirmed). Artists will be fully acknowledged, and further details will be shared soon.
Theme: 20 Years of EstuaryWatch
Submissions might explore:
• Estuaries you’ve monitored, loved, or learned from
• Life by the water — people, plants, animals, tides, seasons
• Citizen science in action
• Change over time (or what stays the same)
• Connection, care, and community
• Reflections on 20 years of EstuaryWatch
What you can submit
Works can be new or existing, provided they align with the theme. You don’t need to be a “professional” artist — this exhibition is about stories, perspectives, and connection, not perfection. We welcome a wide range of creative responses, including:
• Photography
• Painting or drawing
• Printmaking or mixed media
• Textile or fibre art
• Sculpture (small–medium scale)
• Other creative interpretations

Details: Tender Notice - Relocatable House
South West TAFE has for sale by tender one dwelling currently being constructed as part of our Pre-apprentice training program at the Sherwood Park Campus. House will be available for removal by Friday 31st July 2026.
The house is constructed of timber cladding with Colorbond roofing, fitted with Stanford four-panel doors, cathedral ceilings, skirting and architraves and has been built with a view to relocate to a permanent site.
It is suitable for use as a permanent residence, holiday accommodation or office building.

Hospital redevelopment - clinical building (various opportunities)

Tenders.Net #: 1515571
Agency: Hansen Yuncken Pty Ltd
Location: VIC
Closing: 20/11/2027
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*

NOTE: Opportunity details & work packages last updated on 20/04/2026.

Peter McCracken
Regional Manager Barwon Region
M: +61 407 505 477
E: pmccracken@icnvic.org.au
Details: Warrnambool Base Hospital Redevelopment (WBHR) - Main Works - Clinical Building
Overview
The Victorian Government (VHBA & South West Health Care) is investing to deliver the Warrnambool Base Hospital Redevelopment. New multi-storey clinical services building will deliver a bigger emergency department, more operating theatres, an extra 22 inpatient beds and dedicated areas for medical imaging and dialysis.
The proposed Clinic Building will be located on the western side of the existing campus in Warrnambool and comprises of a new 15,000 sqm which will include an Inpatient Ward, Paediatric Ward, New Operating Theatre Department, New Central Sterilising Supply Department (CSSD), New Emergency Department, New Medical Imaging Department, New Renal Department, New Logistics Loading Dock and BOH facilities. Works required to complete the project will include but not limited to Structure, Bulk Earth Works & Civil, Piling, Precast, Façade, Mechanical, electrical, Hydraulic, Fire (Protection & Detection).
The project is due for completion by 20th November 2027
===
WORK PACKAGES

Internal glazed Partitions - 20 Nov 2027
Door Hardware - 29 Jul 2026
carpentry - 29 Jul 2026
Metalwork - 29 Jul 2026

waterproofing - 29 Jul 2026

Details: PROMSA26061 - Supply & install New Reverse Cycle Air- conditioning - Hilton Primary School
Programmed facility management Pty Ltd (PFM) on behalf of Department of Housing and Works (DHW) and Department of Education (DOE) are seeking tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors to undertake a supply & installation of new reverse cycle air-conditioning replacing evaporative units at Hilton Primary School.
The Work includes but is not limited to the supply and install of inverter type split air-conditioners to each classroom currently serviced by the evaporative air-conditioners. Decommission and removal of existing gas heaters and make good.
Once award is issued, tenderer to commence procurement of materials. Please note that all work is scheduled for completion during the July and/or Sept-Oct school term break.

Catering services (panel)

Tenders.Net #: 1602402
Agency: Edith Cowan University (ECU)
Location: WA
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 02:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: NOTE: Opportunity details updated.

Nirit Marom
Phone: (08) 6304 3745
n.marom@ecu.edu.au

Please be advised that all interested parties must first complete and submit a Pre-Qualification Questionnaire (PQQ). Only respondents who are assessed as Qualified through this process will have the ability to submit their responses within the eProcure platform.
Details: 260402 (GEN/260402) - Catering services Panel Request for Proposal (RFP).
Edith Cowan University (ECU) is seeking responses to this Request for Proposal (RFP) from suitable, experience and professional Catering services.
The objective of this Request for Proposal is to establish a multi-supplier Catering services panel that provides the University with access to capable, compliant, and value for money catering services. The panel will support events of varying size, complexity, and profile, including (but not limited to) high value and high profile University functions.
Edith Cowan University (ECU) hosts a diverse range of events throughout the year, including academic functions, corporate engagements, student activities, cultural events, and high-profile stakeholder occasions. Catering services play a critical role in supporting these events and in upholding the University’s reputation for quality, professionalism, and hospitality.
To meet ongoing and future catering requirements, the University is seeking to establish a panel of suitably qualified and experienced Catering Service Providers. This panel will enable the University to efficiently engage reliable suppliers who can deliver consistent, high-quality catering services across a variety of event types, sizes, and formats.
This Request for Proposal (RFP) invites submissions from providers who demonstrate the capability, capacity, and commitment to meet the University’s standards, compliance requirements, and operational needs.  
The vendors will be required to:
- Support events of varying size, complexity, and profile, including high value and high-profile University functions.
- Enable access to a range of catering styles and service models, from simple refreshments to full-service catering.
- Ensure compliance with relevant University policies, food safety regulations, workplace health and safety requirements, and sustainability expectations.
Requirements: 
- Provision of light refreshments, including tea, coffee, and morning and afternoon tea
- Corporate and business lunch catering, including boxed lunches and buffet style offerings
- Catering for formal functions and special events
- Accommodation of dietary, allergen, and cultural requirements (including, but not limited to, vegetarian, vegan, halal, and gluten-free options)
- Compliance with food safety standards and the delivery of consistent quality and reliable service
- Delivery, setup, and pack down of catering services as required
- Provision of sustainable packaging solutions and food waste minimisation initiatives (desirable).
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Details: MCB20252609 - Cleaning services
The Metropolitan Cemeteries Board (MCB) requires a suitably qualified, experienced and licensed organisation(s) for the provision of cleaning and hygiene services for MCB premises. These premises are:
a. Pinnaroo Valley Memorial Park;
b. Karrakatta Cemetery;
c. Fremantle Cemetery;
d. Rockingham Regional Memorial Park;
e. Guildford Cemetery; and
f. Midland Cemetery.
The Contractor must employ (not subcontract) staff to carry out the provisions of the Specifications outlined in the Request
The MCB reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to award all or part of this Contract and may increase or decrease the scope of services required based on operational requirements.
The MCB reserves the right, at its sole discretion, to add additional buildings or extra sites to the Contract in consultation with the Contractor following which an amended price may be submitted.

Details: RFP82025FF. Ground-based Fertilising services.
The FPC seeks to establish a contract for the provision of bulk ground-based fertiliser application services on the FPC's newly established and recently thinned pine plantations located throughout the south-west of Western Australia from the nearest of two nominated depots (Bunbury and Albany).
The fertiliser must be protected from the weather during transit and whilst stored at the application site.
Bulk haulage distance is generally up to 150 kilometres from a fertiliser depot to the application site.

Details: PCS-03867 - CDR Website User Research and Usability Testing services
Requirements
Treasury is seeking an experienced provider to deliver user research and usability testing services to inform improvements to the Consumer Data Right (CDR) website, cdr.gov.au.
The engagement will involve planning and conducting qualitative user research and usability testing with identified user groups, analysing findings, and providing evidence‑based insights and recommendations to support future website design and content decisions. The work will include user recruitment, research activities, prototype development and testing, and reporting of findings.
The services are expected to be delivered in phases over a short engagement period and must align with Australian Government digital standards, including accessibility requirements. Further details on scope, deliverables, evaluation criteria, and response requirements are set out in the Request for Quote documentation.

Grant

Tenders.Net #: 1602725
Agency: Australia-Japan Foundation
Location: ALL
Closing: 02/06/2026 - 02:00 pm (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: Australia-Japan Foundation
E: ajf.australia@dfat.gov.au
Details: GO8383 - Australia-Japan Foundation 2026-27 Grant Round
The Australia-Japan Foundation (AJF) advances Australia's engagement with Japan by supporting people-to-people links through programs and partnerships aligned with the Australian Government's priorities.
The AJF 2026-27 Grant Round invites grant applications for projects in the following priority areas:
• activities that promote cooperation on bilateral, economic security and broader geostrategic issues
• activities that expand Japanese literacy in Australia and/or an understanding of Australia in Japan
• arts, culture, sport, and youth engagement
• activities that advance cooperation in key emerging sectors, including energy transition, critical minerals, resilient supply chains, agriculture, artificial intelligence, robotics, cybersecurity and advanced technologies.
The intended outcomes of the grant round are:
• delivery of impactful and well-promoted activities that catalyse new, and deepen existing, bilateral, institutional and community partnerships and collaborations based on shared strategic goals.
• increased public engagement and understanding and awareness of shared interests between Australia and Japan.
• increased recognition in Japan of Australian excellence and expertise.
• greater involvement of emerging leaders and youth in shaping the future of bilateral cooperation.
• visible inclusion of diverse voices and communities.
• creation of digital assets that can be used to promote the activity and contribute to AJF’s broader communications efforts.
Total Amount Available (AUD): $850,000.00
Estimated Grant Value (AUD): From $50,000.00 to $150,000.00
