UGV to support robotic autonomous system

Tenders.Net #: 1602712
Agency: Department of Defence - DSRG
Location: ALL
Closing: 15/05/2026 - 10:00 am (ACT Local Time)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Click here to make an electronic response*
Contact: NOTE: This information is subject to change and should be verified through the AusTender Website.

Deputy Director Land Combat Vehicle Program
E: ahq.lcvp@defence.gov.au
Details: LCD-26-0009 - UGV to support Robotic Autonomous System
Trial / Test & Evaluation Requirements RAS Breaching
1.Purpose
This procurement is for a trial to evaluate Robotic and Autonomous Systems (RAS) solutions capable of augmenting under armour breaching to reduce risk, accelerate tempo, and enhance persistence in contested environments.
Participation does not constitute a commitment to purchase.
2.Background
Breaching under fire remains one of the most hazardous land combat tasks, exposed to direct and indirect fires, explosive hazards, obstacles, and potential electronic warfare interference. RAS technologies have the potential to reduce human exposure, accelerate breach timelines, and enable human machine teaming. Experimentation is required to validate concepts and inform requirements for future acquisition and Army force design.
3.Trial Objectives
Assess whether RAS solutions can augment under armour breaching, including obstacle reduction, wire breaching, and lane proofing.
Validate RAS performance in contested environments including GPS/EW degradation and UAS dense airspace. Evaluate integration with existing C2 systems.
Determine COTS/MOTS maturity, safety, interoperability, and feasibility of service introduction within 24 months.
4.Scope of Trial
Location: Conducted within Army training areas.
Window: Delivery in Q2 2026 for trial execution in Q3 2026. Activities:
o Breach under fire evolutions (wire/obstacle reduction, lane marking, proofing).
o Integration with combat engineer force elements .
5. Minimum Capability Requirements
- Provide a RAS platform/subsystem that augments under armour breaching including obstacle and wire reduction. Mandatory
- Be COTS/MOTS with feasible service introduction within 24 months, including safety documentation and compliance. Mandatory
6. Trial Scenarios
UGV enabled breach force as part of a deliberate breach scenario. Breaching of dummy minefields
Breaching of wire obstacles
Breaching of obstacles in the urban environment proofing of lanes through obstacles
Marking of lanes through obstacles
7. Evaluation Methodology
Structured mission threads: breach tasks, manoeuvre support, sustainment.
Measured performance: time to breach, obstacle reduction effectiveness, lane proof accuracy, comms resilience, and safe state behaviours.
After action review supported by system logs, ISR footage, and user debriefs.
9. Data Collection Requirements Usage logs, telemetry, sensor feeds.
Communication status, EW impact, disruptions.
Failures/abort events, maintenance actions, setup/pack down times. Survey/interview user feedback and ISR/UGV/UAS video records.
10. Supplier Support Requirements Operator and maintainer training materials. Provision of spares and tools.
The Contractor shall provide technical support services sufficient to meet training requirements and to enable effective operational use of the Uncrewed Ground Vehicle (UGV).
Ability for Defence apply additive manufacturing methods to spares to assure supply chains. Fault response times consistent with activity tempo.
Safety case, documentation, configuration control.
Break-fix support to maintain system availability and ensure UGVs remain operational throughout test, trial, and evaluation activities in September 2026 for approx. 5 days.
11. Deliverables
RAS breaching systems and required accessories.
Safety documentation, operator/maintainer manuals, SDS, configuration lists. Training packages, job aids, reference materials.
12. Risk & Compliance
Compliance with Defence safety, standards, and autonomous system policies. Safe state behaviours during loss of comms or EW overload.
Supplier liability and insurances appropriate for destructive trials.
13. Assumptions
Army provides range access and safety governance.
Suppliers provide on site reps and spares to sustain trial tempo.
14. Future Procurement Statement. Participation in this trial does not guarantee nor provide advantage in any future procurement.
Other Instructions:
conditions for Participation:
Suppliers must be able to deliver the proposed system no later than 29 June 2026 to ensure participation in the scheduled Army trial. Delivery after this date will render the supplier unable to meet trial requirements.
Timeframe for Delivery:
NLT 29 June 2026

ASCA Innovation Challenges
E: asca.innovationchallenges@defence.gov.au
Details: ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-AN2 - Advance Notice - ASCA Pitch Day 2026 - Decoy, Deception, Camouflage and Concealment
The Advanced Strategic Capabilities Accelerator’s (ASCA) mission is to accelerate the development and transition of asymmetric capabilities to the Australian Defence Force through innovation, in order to meet Defence priority needs.
ASCA's Pitch Days provide industry and academia the opportunity to present their ideas to Defence, ensuring Defence remains aware of new capabilities and innovations.
This advance notice is for the second of two Pitch Days in 2026, and will be an RFI on new capabilities and innovations that enable agile signature management through decoy, deception, camouflage and/or concealment.
Responses to the RFI describing such capabilities and innovations - dubbed 'proposed solutions' - must address the Challenge Statement that fall under this theme of 'Decoy, Deception, Camouflage and Concealment'.
Respondents to the RFI that propose promising solutions (as assessed by a panel of Defence subject matter experts) will be invited to pitch to an audience of senior Defence officials, other valued guests and members of the broader defence ecosystem at the Pitch Day event, that will coincide with the Land Forces International Land Defence Exposition 2026 in Perth, in October.
Further information on this upcoming RFI, including the Challenge Statement, assessment criteria and Pitch Day event, will be made available from May 2026.
Visit the ASCA website: www.asca.gov.au/opportunities
This is an advance notice for an upcoming Request For Information (RFI). The 'Close Date & Time' applies to the notice and does not apply to the RFI. The closing date and time of the RFI will be disclosed once the RFI has been released.

Research - pasture seeds program

Tenders.Net #: 1602696
Agency: AgriFutures Australia
Location: ALL
Closing: 27/07/2026 - 12:00 pm (AEST)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Amy La
Manager, Levied Industries
E: pastureseeds@agrifutures.com.au

End of period for questions or requests for information: 20 May 2026, 12PM (AEST)

Open Call – applications close: 25 May 2026, 12PM (AEST)
Open Call – Full Research Proposal (FRP) applications open: 22 June 2026, 12PM (AEST)
Open Call – FRP applications close: 27 July 2026, 12PM (AEST)
Details: Open Call: Pasture Seeds Program 2026-27
AgriFutures Australia investments are driven by RD&E Plans that address the needs of industries and stakeholders, and ensure our priorities are industry and government driven. Our aim is to deliver research and innovation that aims to give our levy payers real returns, as well as prepare them to thrive into the future.
The goal of the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program is to support a thriving and collaborative Australian certified temperate pasture seeds industry. To ensure the Program goal is achieved, AgriFutures is seeking research proposals to address the investment priorities within the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program RD&E Plan 2023-2028.
Background
AgriFutures Australia welcomes Preliminary Research Proposals, that address one or more of the strategies within the R&D objectives for the industry and outlined in the AgriFutures Pasture Seed Program RD&E Plan 2023-2028.
The RD&E Plan is structured around three key priority areas:
1. Supporting production systems and agronomic advancements
2. Developing people
3. Connecting the value chain
As a vital component of all animal production systems, the Pasture Seeds Program operates at a strategic level to ensure investments demonstrate robust value for money while achieving timely and appropriate outcomes for the pasture seeds industry.
We also support and encourage proposals that demonstrate collaboration with animal production industries and projects or short-term support for existing and relevant research relevant to the pasture seeds industry objectives.
Proposals must address strategies, consider the justification, be linked to an indicative activity, and deliver on a Key Performance Indicator (KPI) listed within each priority of the RD&E Plan.

Details: LH-05757 - Minimum of 3 Senior Software Engineer
The Archer IRM SMEs support the existing Archer platform and associated solutions, while undertaking development activities to further enhance or build upon capabilities. This may include:
1. Design and development of new solutions
2. Enhancements to existing solutions
3. Patching, maintenance and defect remediation
4. Testing
Key duties and responsibilities
Duties may include some or all of the following:
• Work within an existing project team, following an agile delivery model using the JIRA platform for backlog management.
• Elicit, refine and document user requirements into user stories to support development.
• Prepare functional solution design documentation to support the delivery of each development sprint. Development sprints are planned by the project team, and this resource will be expected to contribute to that planning.
• Configure the Archer IRM solution to meet business requirements, including completing system integrations between Archer and other existing platforms.
• Contribute to test planning and undertake unit testing prior to promoting changes to Test.
• Showcase sprint outcomes to the business owner and Senior Responsible officer.
• Support production release activities outside of core business hours.
Knowledge/Qualifications
• Protective security Policy Framework (PSPF)
• Information security Manual (ISM)
• Essential 8 Strategies
• National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
Technical Skills (cont).
• Experience reconciling and performance tuning large datasets on a daily basis (tens of millions of records) in Archer IRM
• Experience managing integrations between Archer IRM and multiple upstream/downstream sources (including via REST APIs)
• Strong understanding of the NIST NVD framework (CVE, CPE, CWE, CISA-KEVC)

Details: LH-06185 - 1 Systems Analyst
The Systems Analyst in the T2B Future Planning section collaborates with business stakeholders and technical teams to gather, analyse, and translate integration requirements into effective solutions. Responsibilities include systems analysis, solution design, overseeing implementation, testing, documentation, issue resolution, and fostering continuous improvement. This pivotal position bridges the gap between business needs and technical execution, ensuring seamless connectivity of on-premises and cloud-based enterprise applications using Microsoft Azure tools, ultimately enhancing operational efficiency and innovation within our organisation.
The candidate must hold an active Baseline security clearance prior to commencement.
Key duties and responsibilities
Role will be responsible for, but not limited to:
• Leading the business analysis activities in a multi-disciplinary agile delivery team in pre-discovery, discovery and design and delivery.
• Plan and document user interviews, understand and document both high level and detailed business problems, and analyse root causes.
• Design and facilitate workshops and interviews that include a variety of internal and external stakeholders across all levels.
• Managing stakeholder groups to develop a partnership with the business and foster a productive working environment.
• Transform defined business needs into outcomes and problem statements for large scale Government applications and complex systems.
• Communicate well, using professional judgement to evaluate risks and in the context of a complex and changing environment.
• Foster a positive workplace culture.
• Assist in mentoring and upskilling less experienced team members.
• Provide analysis on complex issues and contribute to the management, preparation and coordination of policy formulation and/or project management.
• Perform additional duties or assume responsibility of functions as directed from time to time.

Details: PCS-03839 - Support and Development services of ASIC’s Internal Evolve and GTR applications
ASIC is seeking to engage a suitably qualified and experienced Seller to provide ongoing support and consultancy services for its internal Evolve Platform and its internal Granular Time Recording (GTR) system.
GTR is a business‑critical internal time recording platform that operates on ASIC’s proprietary Evolve Platform.
The required services include:
• Day‑to‑day operational support of the GTR system
• Specialist consultancy services to support system stability, performance, and business needs
• Occasional code maintenance, defect remediation, and minor development activities, as required to maintain and enhance the platform
• Knowledge of, and experience working with, proprietary platforms similar to Evolve.
The Seller will be expected to work collaboratively with ASIC stakeholders to ensure continuity of the GTR system, effective issue-resolution, and controlled delivery of enhancements in line with ASIC governance, security, and change management requirements.
For more information refer to the SOR

Details: PCS-03862 - DAFF Microsoft Teams Rooms and Digital Signage services
The Department of Agriculture, Fisheries and Forestry (hereinafter, ‘the Department’ or "DAFF”) is looking to engage an experienced Service Provider to provide Audio Visual and Video Conferencing Platform and Support services to the Department. The required outcome is to achieve and implement a comprehensive end-to-end set of architectural and support services that meets the Department’s needs for efficient, reliable, and high quality in-room Audio Visual and Video Conferencing Solutions, including Digital Signage. The successful Service Provider will work collaboratively as part of a mixed team of Departmental staff and other Service Partners utilising a range of technologies focused on Microsoft Teams Rooms (MTR) and Digital Signage capability (the “platform”).
As key examples:
1. The Department’s Managed Network services (MNS) provider is responsible for setting up network VLANs and IP address allocations during the installation of some platform components.
2. The Department’s End User Productivity and Service Desk (EUPSD) provider is responsible for managing Microsoft 365 products, including MS Teams, user and room accounts.

Details: Seeking Expressions of Interest
Rashnyl Prasad and Michael Brereton as Receivers and Managers of The BIMM Group Pty Ltd (Receivers & Managers Appointed) (In Liquidation), and Andrew Scott and Melissa Humann as Liquidators of Unlabelled Australia Limited (In Liquidation), invite expressions of interest for the acquisition of selected business assets of the companies.
This is an opportunity to acquire ownership of necessary intellectual property and associated assets in the well-established consumer skin care market. A purchaser will have the opportunity to recommence operations of a locally sourced, natural skin care range, subject to securing the required approvals.
Business assets available for acquisition include:
• Intellectual property, including high-quality, product formulations for hand and body wash and soap items branded ‘Unlabelled'.
• The registered ‘Unlabelled’ trademark.
• Finished goods inventory.
• Development work relating to additional products not yet in production; and
• Established online retail sales systems.
The ‘Unlabelled' product range has historically been manufactured by Unlabelled Australia Limited, The BIMM Group's subsidiary and registered charity, with distribution via Coles and Chemist Warehouse stores, as well as direct-to-customer channels.
The sale process is being conducted on an as is, where is basis.

Erosion & carbon benefits of buffel grass

Tenders.Net #: 1602591
Agency: Meat & Livestock Australia
Location: ALL
Closing: 01/05/2026 - 11:59 pm (AEDT)
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: If you have questions about this tender, please contact:
Alister Hawksford
Program Manager – Sustainability Credentials
E: ahawksford@mla.com.au
M: 0423 659 140
Details: Erosion and Carbon Benefits of Buffel Grass
Buffel grass pastures are of significant importance to red meat production in both northern and central Australia. Studies have also shown that cattle perform well on buffel-based pastures. As a persistent groundcover, buffel can rapidly re establish after rain or fire, stabilise soils and reduce dust/erosion; these attributes are valued by producers and land management organisations when rehabilitating degraded rangelands. However, recent nomination of buffel grass as a potential Weed of National Significance (WONS) has raised significant concerns among the many producers reliant on buffel.
MLA is seeking applications from organisations or consortiums to conduct a project that defines the role buffel grass plays in reducing soil erosion, and increasing carbon sequestration, in Australian red meat production zones. This will complement an economic benefit assessment being undertaken in parallel.
Applications should meet the requirements specified in the tender document, and include a completed preliminary application form available on the Project application guidelines and forms website.

Details: PCS-03837 - Secure Internet Gateway (SIG) Penetration Testing services
Requirements
The successful supplier will deliver a comprehensive security assessment of CASA’s newly built and self-hosted Secure Internet Gateway (SIG). The objective is to identify vulnerabilities, evaluate the effectiveness of security controls, and provide actionable recommendations to strengthen the overall security posture of the gateway environment.
The engagement will include:
• Offensive security testing from both external (unauthenticated) and internal perspectives, including simulated attack scenarios such as lateral movement under an assumed compromise.
• Secure configuration review of gateway technologies and key supporting systems.
• Secure code review of all in-scope Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) relevant to the gateway services and secure administration.
The successful supplier must provide:
• Detailed test plans, including the proposed methodology, detailed scope, timelines, resourcing, and identification of key stakeholders.
• Comprehensive test reports that clearly document findings, vulnerability ratings (using OWASP, CVSS, or another recognised standard), potential business/operational impact, and prioritised remediation recommendations.
• Experience in testing Microsoft Azure cloud-native environments and virtual appliance-based firewall integrations based on Microsoft’s recommended landing zone hub and spoke architecture.
• Experience with Zero Trust architecture assurance or related security transformation programs.
• Demonstrated recent experience in delivering penetration testing services for government or similarly regulated organisations, including offensive security testing, secure configuration reviews, and Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) assessments for modern cloud native and hybrid scenarios.
• Provide examples for each.
• Ability to deliver all required artefacts, including a detailed test plan, comprehensive test reports with vulnerability ratings (using OWASP, CVSS or equivalent).
• Team members that must hold relevant industry certifications (e.g., OSCP, OSCE, CREST or equivalent) and possess demonstrable experience conducting penetration testing and security assessments across cloud-native and hybrid networks in a federal government environment.
Demonstrated sound understanding of Australian government protective security requirements. The supplier is to provide its proposed resourcing for the engagement, inclusive of CVs, and references from Australian federal government customers.
The supplier will be required to adhere to strict confidentiality and security protocols throughout the engagement.
Prior work in aviation, critical infrastructure, or other highly controlled regulatory environments is desirable.

Agency: Waikato Regional Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 08/05/2026 - 11:55 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: If you are interested in being considered, you will need to apply via email DLC.Application@waidc.govt.nz

Please include a recent CV and/or cover letter outlining why you'd like to be considered.
Details: Expression of Interest - District Licensing Committee
The Opportunity
The Waikato District Council is calling for applications from persons interested in considering and determining alcohol licensing decisions to be part of the Waikato District Licensing Committee (DLC).
Expressions of interest are invited from suitable persons for consideration for appointment as DLC list members and as a Commissioner/Chairperson.
The District Licensing Committee (DLC) consists of a commissioner and two other Committee Members drawn from a list of members appointed by Waikato District Council.
The DLC is responsible for determining alcohol licenses and manager’s certificates in the Waikato District which cannot be determined on the papers and require a hearing. DLCs are quasi-judicial committees of the council, with decisions appealable to the Alcohol Regulatory and Licensing Authority.
The District Licensing Committee (“DLC”) is an independent statutory body, as legislated in the Sale and Supply of Alcohol Act 2012, responsible for determining alcohol licenses and manager’s certificates in the Waikato District which cannot be determined on the papers and therefore require a hearing.

NOTE: Further details via link provided, pending registration to the principal's tendering portal.
Details: RFx ID : 33945922 - Puke Ariki Museum Roofing Works (Reference # : 26-043)
New Plymouth District Council (NPDC) is seeking an experienced contractor to undertake high quality roofing, building, and painting works, delivering a durable, compliant, and cost effective outcome that protects Puke Ariki Museum’s collections and operations while minimising disruption.
The Principal are looking for a contractor with:
1. Proven experience in delivering similar-scale projects
2. Strong project management skills and ability to work within tight timeframes
3. Excellent health and safety practices, particularly regarding public safety in an operational environment
4. Ability to work collaboratively with council staff
5. Commitment to delivering high-quality work.
NPDC values suppliers who contribute positively to broader community outcomes. We encourage proposals that support local subcontractors and suppliers, strengthen the regional economy, and promote workforce development. The contractor must bring a strong track record of safe delivery, high‑standard workmanship, and the ability to manage projects of this nature efficiently and responsibly.

Kai Ora Fund

Tenders.Net #: 1602743
Agency: Kaipara District Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 17/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: For further details, please refer to the link provided.
Details: Kai Ora Fund
About the Kai Ora Fund
The purpose of the Kai Ora Fund is to enable Northlanders to grow and eat nutritious and sustainably grown local food. The Kai Ora Fund is a small community grants process to support innovative projects that address food security, benefit the wider community and encourage employment and economic development in the region. We want our communities to have an accessible, affordable and safe food supply for current and future generations, and to be resilient to future disruption or unavailability of food due to various risk factors including droughts, fuel shortages and economic instability.
The Kai Ora Fund is a partnership between Mahitahi Hauora, Far North District Council, Te Puni Kōkiri, Northland Inc., Kaipara District Council, Northland District Health Board, and Whangarei District Council.

Notice: Traffic counting service

Tenders.Net #: 1602687
Agency: Hamilton City Council
Location: NZ
Closing: Not Stated
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/
Details: RFx ID : 33939761 - Traffic Counting Service - NOI
This Advanced Notice is being published to inform the market of an upcoming procurement opportunity and to support early market awareness and engagement. No response is required at this stage.
Hamilton City Council is planning a future procurement for Traffic Counting services, supporting the delivery of the city’s annual tube count programme and associated quality assurance activities.
Council is progressing a strategic initiative to become an industry leader in traffic counting and estimation. As part of this programme, we have refined internal processes and engaged specialist expertise to develop a new count framework and to manage the REG Traffic Count Estimation tool. The forthcoming procurement seeks a supplier with proven capability and resilience to deliver high quality traffic count data beginning in the 2026/27 counting season.
Please see the attachment for further information on this future procurement.

Renewals project at facility

Tenders.Net #: 1602329
Agency: Dunedin City Council
Location: NZ
Closing: 21/05/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: http://www.gets.govt.nz/

NOTE: Opportunity details updated.
Details: RFx ID: 33935206 - Mona Pool Heat Recovery System (Reference #: 12011)
The DCC is undertaking a renewals project at its Moana Pool Aquatics facility.
As part of this project, it has been identified that the existing HVAC & Heat Recovery (HR) system requires some refurbishment to ensure continued performance and provide a comfortable environment for the facilities users.
DCC is seeking an appropriately experienced contracting partner to deliver the upgrade works on a design and build (D&B) basis.
The proposed Main Hall & Dive Pool HR system will provide operational energy, cost and emissions reductions compared to current operations.
Continuity of service is essential to the Moana Pool.
• The associated works will need to be staged and closely coordinated with the pool staff and the facility Events Schedule.
• The pool will need to remain open during normal operational hours 5:30am to 9:30pm.
• This may require some temporary ventilation when individual HVAC systems are down.

Project Title: Azerbaijan Railways Electrification, Signalling and Telecommunication Project (Bilajari-Yalama)
Contract No. and Title: ARESTP-BY-CW-01 – Design and Build of Electrification, Signalling and Telecommunication Works of Railway Section Bilajari-Yalama
Project No. 59329-001
The Azerbaijan Railways Closed Joint Stock Company (ADY) (“the Employer”) invites sealed bids from eligible Bidders for the design, construction and completion of Electrification, Signalling and Telecommunication Works of Railway Section Bilajari-Yalama (“the Works”).
The Project forms part of a wider programme to modernize 25 kV AC electrification and signalling and telecommunications systems along the railway sections Bilajari-Yalama and Baku–Boyuk Kesik, which are key elements of Azerbaijan’s north–south and east–west rail corridors serving ADY’s international freight operations.

Market sounding - Solar PV, battery storage & electric heating systems incl insulation / ventilation

Tenders.Net #: 1602757
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 25/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Interested suppliers are requested to submit information through the provided Google Form via link provided.

Information requested include the following:
• Company Profile
• Relevant Experience
• Technical & Services Delivery Capability
• Carbon Credit Certification
• Labor and Social Compliance
• Delivery and Scalability
• Indicative Cost and Pricing Information
Details: Mongolia
---
Project Title: Mongolia: Coal to Solar Transition in Ger Districts of Ulaanbaatar City
RFI Title: Market Sounding for the Supply and Installation of Distributed Solar PV, Battery Storage, and Electric Heating Systems, Including Insulation and Ventilation
This Request for Information (RFI) is issued to gather information from potential suppliers for the provision and installation of solar-powered heating systems with battery storage in Mongolia. The requirement is intended as a pilot covering approximately 330 household systems (kits), with potential for future scale-up.
Objective
The objective of this RFI is to assess market capability and supplier capacity, determine supplier experience in similar and cold-climate applications, and evaluate the ability of suppliers to support renewable energy credits or similar mechanisms.
Scope Overview
The project is envisaged to be implemented as a complete system, including renewable energy generation, energy efficiency measures, carbon credit certification services, and all associated components. This RFI seeks to understand market capability, including suppliers that can provide full systems or specific components/services. The scope of services includes the following:
• Converting solar energy into electrical power.
• Supplying electricity for heating with thermal storage.
• Ensuring reliable operation through battery energy storage.
• Incorporating adequate insulation and ventilation components for thermal efficiency, in compliance with UNICEF Core Housing and Infrastructure Program (CHIP) standards
• Emission reduction management and monitoring platform, comprising IOT sensors and centrally controlled system.
• Establishing carbon credit certification readiness, including baseline emissions assessment, monitoring, reporting, and verification (MRV) protocols aligned with recognized standards, to enable generation and monetization of carbon credits.
• Providing all associated components, installation, testing, and commissioning, ensuring system performance, compliance, and readiness for operation and environmental certification.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Support on issuance of infrastructure project bond issuance

Tenders.Net #: 1602735
Agency: Cambodia Australia Partnership for Resilient Economic Development (CAPRED)
Location: O/S
Closing: 15/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: CAPRED is available to answer questions or provide more details on this call for application. Please email procurement@capred.org with “Pilot Demonstration of ESCO Model - Enquiry” in the subject line.
Details: Cambodia
---
CFA25-053 - Requests for Proposals - Support on Issuance of Infrastructure Project Bond Issuance
Project Name: Increase of Infrastructure Bond Issuance Through Issuance Support
Domain Name: Infrastructure
Program Duration: November 2025 – June 2027
About the Support on Issuance of Infrastructure Project Bond
Cambodia's LDC graduation, aimed for 2029, will reduce its ability to access concessional finance. Continuous development and improvement in regulations and market functions, along with successful issuances of different types of securities, have enabled Cambodia's capital market to become a platform ready for raising capital much needed for continued development. It is a place where the private sector can access large-scale and long-term financing suitable for infrastructure projects.
Despite the readiness and rapid growth in bond issuance, the market's breadth and depth remain limited with a small number of issuances per year and an inactive secondary market. Lack of awareness among potential issuers of the financing opportunity and issuance process, limited experience and network of key market actors, and high upfront issuance costs compared to bank financing are limiting Cambodia from seeing a higher number of successful issuances and market deepening, despite the potentials and market demand.
Building on market momentum, CAPRED will provide issuance support to stimulate more bond issuances for infrastructure projects to help the market reach the scale that enables this relatively new financing channel for Cambodia to become a familiar mode of financing, deepen the market, and increase access to sustainable financing for Cambodia's infrastructure development.
The objectives of this program are as follows:
• Build on the momentum of developed rules and regulations and first successful cases for more infrastructure bond issuances to reach a critical mass.
• Increase Cambodia's capacity to access domestic and international capital for large-scale infrastructure projects, expanding Cambodia's financing channels with capital market as an alternative financing approach to prepare for LDC graduation.

ESCO pilot demonstration program

Tenders.Net #: 1602733
Agency: Cambodia Australia Partnership for Resilient Economic Development (CAPRED)
Location: O/S
Closing: 31/12/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: CAPRED is available to answer questions or provide more details on this call for application. Please email procurement@capred.org with “Pilot Demonstration of ESCO Model- Enquiry” in the subject line.
Details: Cambodia
---
CFA25-056 - Call for Application to Participate in the ESCO Pilot Demonstration Program
About the ESCO Pilot Demonstration
In 2023, the Cambodian government released the National Energy Efficiency Policy (NEEP). One of the key strategies within NEEP is fostering the development of the Energy Service Company (ESCO) market. ESCOS offer a unique and attractive business model in which they guarantee a specific level of energy savings over a contracted period.
While ESCOs are a well-established concept globally, Cambodia currently lacks a developed ESCO market. Recognising the potential of ESCOS to contribute to Cambodia's energy efficiency goals, CAPRED aims to pilot potential investment models between ESCOs and businesses. In late March 2024, CAPRED launched a Call for Applications (CFA) or Express of Interest (EOI) from interested businesses or project owners in the rice milling, food processing, and animal farming sectors. Building on the positive response to the initial call, CAPRED is extending the deadline for receiving the applications until 31 December 2026. This is to expand the demonstration of ESCO business model in other high-energy-consuming industries, particularly ice-making factories and hotels.
Project Name: Pilot Demonstration of the Energy Service Company (ESCO) Model for Energy Efficiency services in Cambodia Domain Name: Infrastructure
Target sector: Agriculture production and processing enterprises, ice-making factories and hotels
Program Duration: Jan 2026 - June 2027
Purpose and Scope of the Program
CAPRED is extending this call to invite applications from companies and factories that operate their business in ice-making, hotel rice milling, food processing, and animal farming companies that have proven high energy consumption and are interested in improving energy efficiency to participate in the ESCO Pilot Demonstration Program.
The objectives of this program are as follows:
Demonstrate the viability of ESCO business:
• assess the technical, financial, and economic feasibility of the ESCO model starting with the above sectors and potentially expanding to other major energy-consuming sectors.
• Informing policy: generate insights to inform policy development and regulatory frameworks for ESCO market development in Cambodia.
Target Participants
Applications are open to all Cambodia-based companies - in the rice milling, food processing, animal farming, ice-making and hotel sectors - that meet the following criteria. Women-led businesses are encouraged to apply.

---
Project ID: P508383
Project Title: Building Resilient Digital Infrastructure for Growth - BRIDGE
Notice No: OP00438816
Borrower Bid Reference: NG-FMCIDE-544870-CS-QCBS
Engagement of Environmental and Social Risk management Technical Assistance
The assignment aims to support the PIU to:
Review of Environmental and Social Risk management Instruments:
Support the E&S Unit of the PIU in the review and implementation of all required E&S instruments including site-specific ESIA/ESMPs, Occupational Health and Safety Plans, Resettlement Policy Framework and RAPs, Stakeholder Engagement Plan and GM, Labor management Procedures, GBV/SEA Action Plan, security management Plan, Chance Finds Procedure; etc. ensuring they meet the World Bank and national standards consistent with the ESCP and the Project Operational Manual (POM).
Risk Identification and Mitigation:
Advise the PIU E&S Risk management Unit on identifying and managing environmental and social risks and impacts (including cumulative and climate-related risks) associated with fiber deployment (e.g. land disturbance, vegetation clearing, erosion, pollution, community health and safety, labor influx, GBV/SEA, etc.). Support in the application of the mitigation hierarchy (avoidance, minimization, mitigation) in site selection and design.
Institutional Capacity Building:
Support and assist the PIU and SPV E&S staff in strengthening internal E&S management systems (procedures, checklists, reporting etc.).
Stakeholder and Community Engagement:
Assist the PIU E&S Risk management Unit in executing the Stakeholder Engagement Plan and GM, ensuring inclusive, culturally appropriate consultations, effective communication of project information, diverse languages, accessible format, and safe grievance mechanisms (including SEA/SH-sensitive channels) for all stakeholders, especially vulnerable groups and persons living with disabilities (PWD) in reference to ESIRT procedures.

Functional solution for development of payments

Tenders.Net #: 1602730
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: P: +1 202-458-5858
E: CorporateProcurement@worldbank.org
Details: Colombia
---
0002021827 - Functional Solution for the Development of (G2P) Payments for the Bogotá District
A recent study by the World Bank concluded that the current payment system for the Guaranteed Minimum Income (IMG) in Bogotá has made significant progress in expanding coverage to vulnerable populations, including households in extreme poverty, older adults, and people with disabilities. However, operational complexity and lack of automation in many processes have led to inefficiencies and risks, such as payment errors and confusion among beneficiaries regarding digital wallet options. The analysis highlights that 65% of beneficiaries have more than two digital wallets, and the prevalence of manual procedures increases the risk of fraud and operational bottlenecks. The study emphasizes the need to move towards automated and secure processes, improve data protection policies, and strengthen information campaigns, so beneficiaries understand their options and rights.
To address these challenges, the study evaluates four alternatives for implementing the customer choice model in payment methods: (i) in-person selection at Social Integration Secretariat -SDIS- offices, (ii) confirmation via WhatsApp, (iii) development of a dedicated application by the SDIS, and (iv) integration with the Bre-B digital payments ecosystem. The WhatsApp and Bre-B options were recommended for detailed analysis, as they offer scalable and user-friendly solutions, minimise operational burden, and allow full beneficiary choice across the financial system. In particular, the Bre-B model stands out as the best long-term solution, enabling beneficiaries to select any account within the financial system and facilitating mass payments for government programs. However, successful implementation will require investments in automation and digital infrastructure (both at the Central Bank and Bogota’s level), and regulatory adaptation, but promises greater efficiency, cost savings, and better experience for cash transfers’ beneficiaries.
Based on these results, the World Bank seeks to support SDIS, the Finance Secretariat of the Bogota District (SHD using the Spanish acronym) and the Central Bank of Colombia in: (i) designing and proposing the functional solution for registration of alias associated with the beneficiary's account and deployment of disbursement capabilities leveraging the fast payment system Bre-B, and (ii) undertaking a preliminary analysis of the regulatory, technical and operational aspects of the functional solution, across different implementation pathways.
The study will deliver the documented functional requirements that must be in place for participating agencies’ architecture and operational processes to support the proposed capabilities. These requirements will define the functionalities, processes, data exchanges, controls, and validation mechanisms required for the system to operate effectively. In this context, the functional requirements delivered by this study will serve as a basis for subsequent software development and hardware implementation stages by the relevant agencies, expediting the process to procure and deploy the technical architecture and system components.
The functional requirements will cover two main functionalities:
· Registration of the alias associated with the account in any financial, microfinance, or cooperative entity participating in Bre-B, where the beneficiary wishes to receive social benefits. The functional requirements will document a solution that enables the capture of beneficiary choice across multiple access channels and mechanisms.
· facilitating the payment of benefits or subsidies through the Bre-B fast payments ecosystem by analysing different disbursement models.
The project will use as a reference the technological and functional architecture of the Bogotá District, both the one managed by SDIS for the registration of beneficiaries’ accounts, and SHD for benefits’ delivery through its Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system, discussed in the aforementioned study.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Project ID: P177442
Project Title: Nigeria: State Action on Business Enabling Reforms (SABER) Program
Notice No: OP00439519
Borrower Bid Reference: NG-SABER NPCO-538420-CS-CQS
Engagement Social audit Firm for Saber
The consulting services (“the services”) include to assess and verify the achievement of certain results (described in the ToR) laid out in the Disbursement Linked Indicator (DLI) matrix (and Verification Protocol) in a manner and substance that conforms with guidelines, procedures and proper documentation. Results will be verified through desk reviews, key informant interviews. and physical inspection through field visits to confirm authenticity. This assignment relates only to a section of DLI 1.2 in the Verification Protocol (VP) and DLI Matrix, which requires, among other things, that States demonstrate that the Framework for Responsible and Inclusive Land Intensive Investment, FRILIA, is under satisfactory implementation as evidenced by its application to at least 1 pilot investment materialized that has been approved under the FRILIA process and confirmed in a social audit. The assignment is expected to start in Q2 2026. During the assignment, the selected firm is expected to conduct the social audit for all States participating in the DLI (Disbursement Linked Indicator) and in line with the timelines set out in an audit plan approved by the Program Coordinating Unit.

Childcare facility & operational guidelines assessment

Tenders.Net #: 1602705
Agency: Market Development Facility
Location: O/S
Closing: 06/05/2026 - 05:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Fiji -

Anushma Sharma
E: anushma.sharma-mdf@thepalladiumgroup.com

Send queries to the Contact Person by email on or before Wednesday, 28th April 2026, 5:00pm Fiji Time. Quote the RFQ Title in the subject line.

Register your interest to submit an offer by sending your name, organisation and contact details to the Contact Person by email on or before Wednesday, 28th April 2026, 5:00pm Fiji Time.
Details: Fiji
---
RFQ-FJ26-030_ Technical Consultant – Childcare facility & Operational Guidelines Assessment
Fiji’s Outsourcing services (OS) sector employs over 8,000 people, two-thirds of whom are women. While the sector provides strong employment opportunities for Fiji, staff retention, attendance and productivity has become a growing challenge due to inadequate childcare services. A 2019 International Finance Corporation (IFC) report estimated that employers in Fiji lose up to 12.7 workdays per employee annually due to childcare responsibilities.
MDF has partnered with Packleader Pacific Ltd (PPL) to help pilot an in-house after school facility on their premises in Laucala Beach Estate, Nasinu to address the growing challenges faced by their staff for afterschool childcare support.
PPL, part of the Packleader Group has been operating in Fiji since 2013 offering customer service, relationship management, web hosting, debt collection, and financial reporting to clients in energy, health insurance, telecommunications, property maintenance, and business processing. Their work across key industries such as energy, health, insurance, banking, telecommunications, and chartered accounting reflects a strong alignment with the needs of working families.
MDF and PPL are seeking to engage a suitable Technical Consultant (‘Consultant’) on a short-term basis to assess the facility and operational guidelines for childcare services, and to provide practical recommendations aligned with Australian childcare standards and Fiji’s local compliance requirements.
The objective of this assignment is to assess the Packleader facility and dedicated childcare space of approximately 22 square metres and review the operational guidelines for childcare services on the premises. The assessment will integrate findings from an internal demand survey for childcare services and provide strategic and practical recommendations for implementation.
Scope of services
The Consultant will work closely with MDF and Packleader to deliver the following:
i. Workplan Development
• Develop a detailed workplan outlining timelines, research methodology, stakeholders to consult, and deliverables.
ii. facility Assessments & Operational audit
• Consult with appropriate stakeholders on statutory and regulatory requirements for development and operation of an on-site after school care facility.
• Review existing operational guidelines (policies, SOPs, staffing, safety, hygiene etc.) against Australian childcare standards and Fiji’s local compliance requirements
iii. Demand Survey Integration
• Analyse demand survey findings (capacity gaps, parent preferences, affordability constraints, hours of operation, inclusion needs) and link to facility assessment results.
iv. Gap analysis
• Provide a matrixed gap analysis and recommend short-, medium-, and long-term actions, that include but are not limited to:
a. Upgrades to Policy and/or standard operating procedures (SOP) relating to child-safety policies, staff hiring, incident reporting, and security procedures where applicable. This should align with Australian childcare standards and adapted for Fiji context
b. facility modifications (space per child, safety/egress, sanitation)
c. Staffing & training (ratios/quals per National Quality Standard intent and Fiji expectations).
d. Governance & leadership (quality improvement plans; internal audits; parental engagement).
e. Digital safety protocols (image consent, storage/destruction, CCTV governance)
v. Final report
• Deliver Final Report (executive summary, method, findings, gap analysis, recommendations, tools annexes) and present findings to MDF and Packleader.
This engagement is intended for locally based consultants. Therefore, costs related to in-country visits will not be covered under this assignment.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Ms Celestine Omi
Manager Government Office Development
Department of Finance
Email: celestine_omi@finance.gov.pg
Phone: +675 303 7835

For Further Information: Please refer to NPC Website: www.npc.gov.pg

Tender Document Fee:
A non-refundable fee of (K 2,000.00) for each tender in the form of a bank cheque, deposit
made payable to
National Procurement Commission
Account Number: 7018100276
Bank: Bank South Pacific
Branch: Down Town, Port Moresby
BSP Code Number: 88-294
Swift Code: BOSPDGPM

Bid Security:
• A Re-fundable fee of K10,000.00 Bank Deposit or Bank Guarantee must be submitted with the Bids.
• Bids submitted without a bid security will be rejected after the bids are opened.
• Bid Security for successful bidder shall be retained by the National Procurement Commission.

Bid Validity Period: One Hundred and Twenty Days (120) Days.

Document Availability:
at National Procurement Commission Hela 1 Building, Melanesian Way, Sir John Guise Drive, Waigani. National Capital District

All Proposals should be Addressed and Delivered to:
Mr Joe Sapa
Chief Executive Officer
National Procurement Commission
P.O. Box 6457, Boroko, National Capital District

Jean-Fernand Krou

Details: Cote d'Ivoire
---
Project ID: P177118
Project Title: Cote d'Ivoire water security and Sanitation Support Program
Notice No: OP00429911
Borrower Bid Reference: CI-PASEA-531805-CS-QCBS
Recruitment of a consultant to carry out detailed architectural and technical studies for the development of a water resource management and mobilization center in Eloka, Bingerville
The specific objectives of these studies are:
For the studies:
• The development of the 6-hectare site;
• Architectural design of all buildings (administrative, technical, educational, etc.) and infrastructure (roads and utilities);
Furniture selection;
• IT requirements definition;
• Topographic and geotechnical site surveys;
• Preliminary and detailed design studies (APS and APD) at the construction phase (TCE) level for all buildings (administrative, technical, educational, etc.) with the production of construction drawings;
• Obtaining the preliminary EDGE certificate (green building);
• Obtaining approval of the design plans, construction drawings, and fire safety report from a Construction Risk Standardization Bureau with current ISO certifications and accreditations from insurers.
• Preparation of the building permit application and monitoring its processing with the single point of contact;
For architectural monitoring and control:
• Architectural monitoring of the construction work;
• Monitoring the purchase of equipment for construction, IT, real estate, etc.;
• Monitoring the installation and configuration of the IT infrastructure;
• Monitoring the deployment of furniture and all other equipment;
• Assistance with the awarding of work contracts.

Details: Turkiye
---
Project ID: P509952
Project Title: Istanbul North Rail Crossing Project (INRAIL)
Notice No: OP00436517
Borrower Bid Reference: AYGM-INRAIL-2025-DAN 02
Project management Consulting (PMC) services for the General Directorate of Infrastructure Investments (AYGM)
The consulting services (“the services”) include managing all aspects of the INRAIL Project and strengthening the PIU’s overall project management capacity. The PMC will assist Client with the day-to-day execution, implementation monitoring, performance evaluation, and coordination of the project, , including adherence to all lenders’ fiduciary, environmental and social (E&S), and reporting requirements under the financing agreements. The scope of the services covers, inter alia: (i) procurement and financial management; (ii) contract management; (iii) Environmental, Social, Health and Safety (ESHS) compliance and resettlement, including land acquisition; (iv) stakeholder engagement; and (v) implementation monitoring and reporting.

Sitiveni Tabua
P: 836 0219
E: sitivenit@prb.com.fj

The Terms of Reference (TOR) outlining the requirements for this tender are available on the PRB website: www.prb.com.fj

Submission method: Electronic submissions only (via PRB website)

Tender submissions must be clearly marked:
“ITT 03/2026 - Canteen Space, Naikawakawa Site”

Addressed to:
The General Manager
Public Rental Board
132 Grantham Road
Raiwaqa

PRB is not bound to accept the highest or any tender and reserves the right to reject any or all submissions.
Details: Fiji
---
ITT 03/2026 - Canteen Space at Naikawakawaiex-Sewer) Site, Raiwaqa
The Public Rental Board (the “Board”), established under the Housing (Amendment) Decree 1989, invites submissions from eligible individuals to lease and operate the Canteen Space at the Naikawakawa (Ex-Sever) Site, Raivaqa, Suva.
This opportunity is specifically targeted at low-income households, and applicants must have a total household income below $30,000 per annum.

Lease of office and commercial space

Tenders.Net #: 1602572
Agency: National Fire Authority
Location: O/S
Closing: Not Stated
Documentation: Availability Unknown
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Fiji -

For more information, please contact:
Mr. Puamau Sowane
E: Puamau.sowane@nfa.com.fj
M: 9986314

Mr. Sen Jeet
E: sen.jeet@nfa.com.fj
M: 9986272

Mr. Joel Israel
E: joel.israel@nfa.com.fj
M: 9987501
Details: Fiji
---
Expression of interest - National Fire Authority - TURRET
The National Fire Authority invites Expression of Interest from Government Ministries, reputable Non-Government Organisations. International and Corporate Bodies, Commercial and Business Entitles to participate in the lease of highly secured office and commercial space in Suva's upcoming GREEN BUILDING. This is a rare and exclusive opportunity to partner with National Fire Authority in making your new office with higher views than others in the vicinity.
Situated in the upscale and tranquil area of Samabula North, the NFA Turret is poised to become a new landmark in Suva, conveniently located near key decision-making bodies and the Suva Central Business District. Currently in the planning stages, this building will be one of Suva's 'Green Buildings,’ offering a total of 8.800 square meters of premium lettable space. It features an artistic design and incorporates environmentally friendly elements that positively impact both our health and the natural environment by conserving valuable natural resources.
As a tenant, you will enjoy the advantages of these Green Features:
• Reduced utility expenses due to the efficient utilization of energy, water, and other resources.
• The lighting system will combine natural daylight with energy-efficient lighting solutions.
• To promote the health and safety of Its occupants, the NFA Turret will be equipped with air filters and provide fresh air for each tenant.
• Transportation is convenient, with only a few dollars in taxi fare from the city and within walking distance to the main road.
• Parking at the NFA Turret is ample, as it Is situated away from the city's hustle and bustle, ensuring sufficient parking space for both tenants and customers.
• Leisure and entertainment opportunities abound, as the NFA Turret will feature a first for Fiji: a secured viewing deck that allows visitors to enjoy panoramic views of greater Suva and its surroundings.
• security controlled access system to all floors.
The NFA Turret Is a Class A commercial building that wtN accommodate retail outlets on the ground floor and ten levels of office space, complete with adequate parking facilities- Each floor, offering 800 square meters of office space, can be leased to single or multiple tenants.
The development and professional management of the NFA Turret are being handled by NFA, which boasts over a century of experience in property investment and management, ensuring that the NFA Turret will be well maintained and provide exceptional services. This development wiH establish a new standard in Fiji under the NFA banner, presenting you with an opportunity for a long-term lease.

Security services

Tenders.Net #: 1602566
Agency: Sugar Cane Growers Fund
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026 - 04:00 pm
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Fiji -

For additional information or clarification please contact Ravneel Singh mobile 8935210 or email ravneel.singh@scgf.com.fj

Interested bidders are requested to submit their proposals and documents as per details below:
- Cover Letter: Introduces your company and explicitly expresses interest in the project.
- Company Profile & Experience: Provides details on your firm, including years in business, and highlights similar services completed.
- Technical Resource Capability: details of your total workforce and how this will be implemented.
- Financial Standing: Includes audited financial statements for the last 3 years to demonstrate financial capacity.
- Referees: Provides references from past clients for verification.

The closing date for the above is 30 April 2026 before 4:00pm via email (tender@scgf.com.fj) and/or placed in the tender box provided at our Head Office located at 57 Drasa Avenue road, Lautoka.

Late submission will not be accepted. Previous quotes supplied need to resubmit through the tender process.
Details: Fiji
---
Expression of Interest (004/2026) - security services - Head Office
Sugar Cane Growers Fund [SCGF] established by Act. No. 9 of 1984 enacted by the Parliament of Fiji on 26 July 1984. Its core business is to provide loans to registered sugar cane growers. SCGF also has a subsidiary company; South Pacific Fertilizers Limited, engaged in sale and distribution of fertilizer and weedicide.
SCGF is currently looking for a reputable security Service Provider for our Head Office in Lautoka.

Details: Fiji
---
Request for Tender - Traffic Engineering Consultancy services for Multi-Storey Commercial Development Namaka, Nadi
The Unit Trust of Fiji (UTOF) invites reputable and suitably qualified Traffic Engineering Firms to submit tenders for the provision of Traffic Engineering services for a proposed multi-storey (mid-rise) commercial development in Namaka, Nadi.
Scope of Work:
The appointed firm will be required to undertake a comprehensive Traffic Impact Assessment in accordance with the Terms of Reference (ToR) provided in the Tender Brief. Evaluation of submissions will be based on aggregate scoring across all criteria outlined in the Tender Brief. Prospective firms must demonstrate a strong track record in the design and successful delivery of low to mid-rise office developments, specifically where traffic engineering services have been provided and completed.

Social & behaviour change

Tenders.Net #: 1602466
Agency: WFP
Location: O/S
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Hilal Aytemiz Samrioglu
E: hq.tenders@wfp.org
Details: Multiple destinations
---
HQ26NF063_EOI for Social and Behavior Change
The United Nations World Food Programme hereinafter referred to as the “WFP”, with its Headquarters located in Via C.G. Viola, 68/70, 00148 Rome, Italy is the leading humanitarian organization saving lives and changing lives, delivering food assistance in emergencies and working with communities to improve nutrition and build resilience by assisting almost 100 million people in around 83 countries each year. About 17,000 people work for the organization, most of them in remote areas, directly serving the hungry poor.
The WFP Nutrition & Food Quality Service/HQ seeks to purchase services for Social and Behaviour Change (SBC) programming to support its nutrition, resilience, food security, school‑based, social protection, and emergency response interventions across about 120 countries globally, based on country office’s needs. WFP applies SBC approaches throughout its programmes, with a particular focus on enhancing dietary diversity and improving nutrition outcomes.
To this end, WFP invites qualified institutions, companies, and agencies with the required expertise and experience to establish Long-Term Agreements (LTAs) for the provision of SBC-related services on an as-needed basis. WFP is particularly interested in partnering with suppliers based in the Regions where WFP operates and who possess local presence and a proven understanding of the socio-cultural dynamics of at least one of the geographical areas where WFP operates: Asia, Southeast Asia, Pacific; Middle East; North Africa; Eastern Europe; Central Asia; West Africa; Southern Africa; Eastern Africa; Latin America & the Caribbean.
WFP invites eligible suppliers to express their interest in providing the requested SBC services listed below. Suppliers with demonstrated capacity in one or more of the below listed areas of work are invited to apply the required service.
Design and conduct formative research into behavioural drivers, social norms and contextual factors to inform the SBC programming framework.
Develop theory-driven and evidence-based SBC strategies aligned with programmatic goals and responsive to participating audiences’ needs and preferences, including through participatory co-design processes.
Design and produce culturally appropriate SBC materials, tools and job aids in a range of formats, including print, audio-video and digital formats.
Plan and execute comprehensive SBC programmes, using a range of appropriate approaches such as mass media, digital engagement, individual and group-based interpersonal communication, nudging/boosting, social marketing, social mobilisation and advocacy.
Develop and deliver SBC capacity strengthening initiatives to enable Government and non-Government partners to design and roll-out structured, evidence-based SBC interventions.
Design and execute implementation research, process and/or impact evaluations to test innovative SBC interventions and generate new evidence using a mix of appropriate research methods.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

Tenders.Net #: 1602464
Agency: UNDP
Location: O/S
Closing: 08/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: procurement.ng@undp.org
Details: Nigeria
---
UNDP-NGA-01398 - Long Term Agreement - Provision of Internal Cleaning and Cleaning of Facades
UNDP Nigeria Country Office is seeking to contract a highly competent, skilled and experienced professional Cleaning services company to provide internal cleaning services at UN House Abuja which accommodates a host of United Nations Agencies, Funds and Programmes including but not limited to UNDP, UNDSS, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA, FAO, UN Women, UNIDO, UNODC, UN RCO, UNAIDS, WMO, WIPO, UNHABITAT, UNIC, UNOPS, UNSE4ALL, UN GCO, ILO and UNESCO. This scope project description also extends to other UN Offices within Abuja and other states of the Federation for which additional personnel may be required.
The required services shall consist of routine high-level cleaning and janitorial services of all the buildings and Offices within UN House premises on daily basis during the contract period. The company shall follow global best practice methods/techniques, and use environmentally friendly consumables & products, which are designed to reduce the impact on the environment.

Solarisation of borehole, pump upgrade, transmission & extension of pipelines

Tenders.Net #: 1602461
Agency: UNHCR
Location: O/S
Closing: 10/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: E: ugakasup@unhcr.org
Details: Uganda
---
UNHCR RFP 2726 - Design and Build: Solarisation of Kitoro Borehole, Pump Upgrade of Kavule 2 Borehole, Transmission Pipelines to Malembo and Kagoma Block 10 Storage Tanks, Pipeline Extension to Bukin
The Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) in Kampala, Uganda invites suppliers having expertise in water and sanitation services to make a firm offer for a complete, climate-resilient, and technically sound water supply system comprising: the motorisation of Kitoro Borehole (32 m³/hr) to supply two existing elevated storage tanks currently underserved by five boreholes with a combined capacity of only 24 m³/hr; the upgrade of Kavule 2 Borehole pump from 30 m³/hr to 45 m³/hr to improve service levels; the extension of a distribution pipeline to the Bukinda New Arrivals area (approximately 24 km from the established tapping point) to eliminate emergency water trucking; and the installation of ten tapstands to improve beneficiary access along the network.

Treasury bills

Tenders.Net #: 1602459
Agency: Reserve Bank of Fiji
Location: O/S
Closing: 21/04/2026 - 09:00 am
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: Fiji -

For further information, you can contact the Domestic Markets Unit, Financial Markets Group by telephone on 3313 611 ext. 323/485 or via email: domesticmarkets@rbf.gov.fj

The Central Security Depository (CSD) participants are to submit their bids using the CSD bidding option by 10.00am on Tuesday, 21 April 2026. The non-CSD participants are to email their tender forms to tender-markets@rbf.gov.fj by 9.00am on Tuesday, 21 April 2026, together with evidence of payment for the full face value of the tendered amount through FIJICLEAR to the RBF.


Transfer details for FIJICLEAR are provided below:
SWIFT: RBFJFJFJXXX
Beneficiary Name: Fiji Government Securities Proceeds & Refunds
Beneficiary Account No: 36019
Narration: Purchase of Government Treasury Bills

Once allotment of Treasury Bills has been made, the settlement for successful bids will be done through the FIJICLEAR by 2.00pm on Wednesday, 22 April 2026.
For the non-CSD participants, if the settlement amount for the accepted tender exceeds the face value, the successful non-CSD bidder is to settle the difference by 1.00pm on Tuesday, 21 April 2026.

Prospectus and tender forms are available on the Reserve Bank of Fiji website.
Details: Fiji
---
Treasury Bills
The Reserve Bank of Fiji, on behalf of the Government of the Republic of Fiji, invites tenders for the issue of Treasury Bills up to a limit of $25,000,000 with details below:
Settlement Date: 22 April 2026
ISIN - FJ1497161356 - FJ1556288652
Term - 6 Months - 12 Months
Maturity - 21 Oct 2026 - 21 Apr 2027
Yield Curve as at 31 March 2026 (%) - 0.45% 1.12%
Amount Floated $10.0 million $15.0 million
Minimum (Non-competitive tender)
Non-competitive tenders will be accepted in multiples of $250 for a minimum amount of $250 (face value) up to a maximum amount of $100,000 (face value).
Minimum (Competitive tender)
Competitive tenders will be accepted in multiples of $10,000 (face value) and the minimum tender will be $10,000 (face value).

Security engineering & fire life safety services

Tenders.Net #: 1602366
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 05/05/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 30/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

All requests and questions regarding this solicitation shall be directed to the following designated Procuring Staff Member:
Nikita Rodrigues
E: nrodrigues@worldbank.org

The solicitation may be obtained by sending an email to the designated Procuring Staff Member, referencing the following information:
1. Solicitation Number - RFP 26-0237
2. Company Name
3. Contact Person Name and Title
4. Contact's Email Address

A copy of the solicitation will be sent to organisations that have replied to and are eligible to receive this advertisement.
Details: Multiple Destinations
---
RFP 26-0237 - Framework Agreements for security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services for the WBG offices
The World Bank Group (WBG) owns and leases office space and buildings in more than 175 locations worldwide which are grouped into seven regions as detailed below. The WBG is seeking the services of Physical security & Fire Life Safety Engineering services firm (or “firms”) to perform the work described in this Statement of Work (SoW). The work described in this SoW will be performed in WBG owned or leased spaces in the following regions:
1. Africa East (AFE)
2. Africa West (AFW)
3. East Asia and Pacific (EAP)
4. South Asia (SAR)
5. Europe and Central Asia (ECA)
6. Middle East and North Africa-Afghanistan & Pakistan (MNAAP)
7. Latin America and the Caribbeans (LCR)
8. Service Centre in Chennai, India & Sofia, Bulgaria
For purpose of selection of quantity surveying contractor(s) these regions will be grouped in 4 super regions as follows:
1. AFE and AFW
2. EAP, SAR, and GCSIR
3. ECA and MNAAP
4. LCR
Mandatory Criteria:
Only those Offerors with sufficient experience, the financial strength and stability and the demonstrable technical knowledge will qualify for further consideration. The following mandatory criteria will be applied:
1) The Firm must demonstrate minimum average annual gross revenues of USD 3M over the past three (3) years from services similar to the Statement of Work.
2) The Firm must be legally registered and authorised to provide security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services, have been in business for a minimum of five (5) years, and maintain an office or lawful operational presence in the regions for which you are submitting your proposals.
3) The Firm must demonstrate successful and continuous delivery of security Engineering & Fire Life Safety services over the past five (5) years, directly performed by the Firm.

Development of cybersecurity risk management framework

Tenders.Net #: 1600152
Agency: The World Bank
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 23/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Horacio Parquinio
E: horacio.parquinio@mtc.gov.mz
Details: Mozambique
---
Project ID: P176459
Project Title: Mozambique Digital Acceleration Project
Notice No: OP00432493
Borrower Bid Reference: 127/INTIC/PADIM/QCBC/25
Define map critical information infrastructures and identify cybersecurity risks at a national level and establish a cybersecurity and resilience plan
The objective of this consulting services is to identify and classify the assets and stakeholders of CI/CII, followed by the development of a National Cybersecurity Risk management Framework adapted to the context of Mozambique. The assignment will also aim to:
i) Develop and present the methodology and criteria for the selection of Critical Information Infrastructures (CII);
ii) Identify, classify, and develop a database of all Critical Information Infrastructures (CII) at the national level;
iii) Situational analysis and gap assessment of the cybersecurity risk management practices of Critical Information Infrastructures (CII), conducted as a summary (light-touch) assessment, aimed at supporting the development of the National Cybersecurity Risk management Framework for CIIs;
iv) Develop the National Cybersecurity Risk management Framework for CIIs.

Piloting digital & geospatial tools to strengthen crop production (various services)

Tenders.Net #: 1600042
Agency: Asian Development Bank (ADB)
Location: O/S
Closing: 28/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 21/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

Project Officer
Shingo Kimura
Principal Natural Resources and Agriculture Economist
E: shkimura@adb.org

Contact Person for Inquiries
Tsutomu Koyama
Natural Resources and Agriculture Specialist
E: tskoyama@adb.org
Details: Pakistan
---
TA-10708 REG: Firm: Piloting Digital and Geospatial Tools to Strengthen Sustainable Crop Production (59074-001)
Under ADB TA titled “Accelerating Sustainable and Resilient Transformation of the Rice Sector in Asia”, Pakistan has been selected for pilot demonstrations to (i) support digital monitoring of Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD) practices and (ii) establish a remote sensing–based measurement, reporting, This pilot builds on the promising results of ADB’s 2024 AWD trials in Punjab (Okara and Sheikhupura). It complements the Punjab Agriculture Department’s (PAD) efforts to demonstrate and scale climate-smart irrigation practices. This initiative directly supports Output 1 (scalable innovations) of the TA. The activity will be implemented through a consulting firm, engaged under the guidance of the Asian Development Bank (ADB), with technical inputs from individual consultants and in close coordination with relevant provincial agriculture departments.
The firm will design, test, validate and demonstrate an integrated digital package that combines Smart AWD tubes, satellite/based irrigation scheduling. Key activities include:
(i) Select four pilot rice sites in consultation with the Punjab agriculture department.
(ii) Recruit at least 60 farmers across the sites, covering at least 50 ha.
(iii) Install analogue AWD tubes on all farms and Smart AWD tubes on at least one farm per site; collect paired data for validation.
(iv) Acquire and analyse satellite imagery to derive soil-moisture and irrigation indicators; issue localised advisory (via SMS/WhatsApp), validated against in-field measurements.
(v) Conduct regular field visits to monitor AWD implementation and to collect agronomic, water-use.
(vi) Procure of rentals equipment/services for pilot demonstrations
(vii) Provide training and capacity building for provincial agriculture staff on AWD monitoring.
(viii) Convene a technical workshop including, to present findings and obtain feedback.
(ix) Prepare a comprehensive final report summarising pilot activities, accuracy comparisons (remote-sensing vs. ground data; Smart vs. analogue tubes), capacity-building outcomes, and recommendations for national scale-up.
(x) Analyse the opportunities for carbon credits projects by ADB which benefits farmers and formulate the project if appropriate.

Digital transformation of public procurement

Tenders.Net #: 1599640
Agency: Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD)
Location: O/S
Closing: 28/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Previously advertised as closing 24/04/2026. Please confirm with contact.

E: yoann.untereiner@oecd.org

An online information session to be held online via Zoom on 15th April 2026 at 3pm. For further details, please refer to the link provided.
Details: Austria
---
3414 - Expertise in Digital Transformation of Public Procurement
The Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) is launching this Market Consultation with the objective of identifying a contractor to support a project on digital public procurement for a period of up to 15 months.
This consultation is carried out in the context of the OECD project Digital Transformation of Public Procurement in Austria, funded by the European Commission’s Reform and Investment Task Force (SG REFORM). The project is implemented by the Infrastructure and Public Procurement Division (IPP) of the OECD’s Public Governance Directorate, with the Public Procurement Directorate of Austria (PPDA) at the Ministry of Justice (Bundesministerium für Justiz) as the main beneficiary.
Additional information about the project is available on the project website of the Ministry of Justice here (in German).
Through this consultation, the OECD seeks technical expertise to support the implementation of key project deliverables, in particular the stocktaking report (Output 2) and the technical feasibility study and proof(s) of concept (Output 4). The selected contractor will be expected to provide high-quality technical input, engage in regular coordination meetings with the OECD, PPDA, and other stakeholders, and present findings at workshops and related events.
Individuals and companies can participate in this call for tenders.
