Energy audit & capacity building services

Tenders.Net #: 1598661
Agency: Cowater International / CAPRED
Location: O/S
Closing: 30/04/2026
Documentation: Click here for documents or further information*
Lodgement: Availability Unknown
Contact: NOTE: Opportunity details updated.

Please email procurement@capred.org with the subject line: RFP26-064- Energy Audit and ESCO CB Services - Enquiry, no later than 24 April 2026.

The program will host a Q&A session on 21 April 2026.

Interested bidders may register to join the briefing by emailing procurement@capred.org, no later than 20 April 2026.
Details: Cambodia
---
RFP26-064 - Request for Proposals: Energy audit & ESCO Capacity Building services, Cambodia
CAPRED seeks to engage a qualified consulting firm or consortium to provide technical services to CAPRED's ESCO partners.
The CAPRED facility is Australia's flagship bilateral economic development program in Cambodia, focusing on economic recovery and resilience over the next five years, with a possible three-year extension. The facility supports Cambodia in implementing a range of important economic reforms to support the country's economic transition. These interventions and reforms aim to encourage more productive and inclusive public and private investment, promoting a resilient, inclusive, and sustainable economy.
CAPRED is implementing an Energy Service Company (ESCO) Pilot Demonstration Program, which aims to promote and test ESCO-based investment models for energy efficiency measures in selected energy-intensive sectors, including agriculture production and processing enterprises, ice-making facilities, and hotels. The program is implemented in collaboration with ESCOs and participating facility owners and includes technical support to identify, assess, and develop energy efficiency opportunities for potential investment.
CAPRED seeks to engage a qualified consulting firm or consortium to provide technical services to conduct the detailed energy audits at participating facilities and provide on-the-job coaching to CAPRED's ESCO partners.
The assignment will consist of the following two scopes of work:
Scope 1 - Conduct detailed energy audits for up to five facilities
The consulting firm/consortium shall conduct Level 2 energy audits for up to five facilities in Cambodia, in accordance with recognised international standards and good industry practice (e.g. ISO 50002 or equivalent). The work shall be carried out in the following phases.
Pre-audit phase:
• Confirm scope, methodology, and workplan with CAPRED and facility owners
• Collect and review available facility information, including energy consumption data, production data, equipment inventory, and operational information
• Identify major energy-consuming systems and define measurement requirements
• Prepare audit tools, templates, and site visit schedule
During audit phase:
• Conduct on-site energy assessments of major energy-using systems
• Perform on-site measurements and checks to assess energy consumption and the performance of major energy-using systems
• Analyse energy consumption patterns and establish baseline performance indicators
• Identify potential energy efficiency opportunities and operational improvements
• Discuss preliminary findings with facility representatives and CAPRED
• The audit shall cover major energy-consuming systems as relevant to each facility, including but not limited to electrical systems, motors, refrigeration, HVAC, compressed air, steam systems, lighting, and generators.
Post-audit phase:
• Identify priority measures suitable for ESCO-based investment
• Assess technical and financial feasibility of identified energy efficiency measures
• Estimate potential energy savings, investment costs, indicative payback periods, and associated greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reductions
• Prepare draft and final energy audit reports for each facility
• Present findings and recommendations to facility owners and CAPRED
Scope 2: Provide on-job coaching on energy audit to CAPRED's ESCO partners
The consulting firm/consortium shall provide practical, on-the-job coaching to CAPRED's ESCO partners throughout all phases of the energy audit assignment (pre-audit, during audit, and post-audit) to strengthen their capacity to independently undertake energy audits and develop investment-ready energy efficiency projects.
• Orientation on energy audit methodology, tools, and reporting requirements
• Demonstration of data collection, measurement, and analysis techniques
• Guidance on identifying and prioritising energy efficiency opportunities
• Support in preparing calculations, feasibility analysis, and audit reports
• Ongoing technical guidance during site visits, analysis, and reporting.
TENDERS.NET - http://www.tenders.net

---
Project ID P181962
Project Title Accelerating Project preparation with CAIXA for electromobility, digital and sustainable housing development
Notice No OP00435646
Borrower Bid Reference BR-GESUB - CAIXA-518417-CS-SMC-PRE-VIAB
Hiring of consulting services to support the structuring of pre-feasibility projects with two potential clients (states and municipalities) of the CAIXA credit line.
Business consulting services include the development of studies and methodologies that support the evaluation and pre-feasibility of technical, economic, financial, legal, and socio-environmental aspects of public transport decarbonisation projects, to be applied to up to three public entities. The consulting services should structure and standardise analysis methods, support the development of pilot projects, develop technical instruments and evaluation tools, and systematise quality criteria and minimum requirements for the eligibility of electromobility initiatives financed by CAIXA.

Details: Georgia
---
LOAN 59441-001 GEO: Tbilisi Bypass Project (Lots 4 and 5) - TBP/CS/QCBS-01 Consulting services for Construction Supervision of Tbilisi Bypass Lots 4 and 5
The Government of Georgia has applied for loan financing from ADB for the project. The executing agency for the project will be the Ministry of Infrastructure (MOI) and the implementing agency will be its Roads Department (RD). RD will be the Employer for the civil works contracts.
The project will be implemented through two civil works contracts for Tbilisi Bypass lot 4 and lot 5. The RD is procuring these contracts under the open competitive bidding procedures of Procurement Regulations for ADB Borrowers. The contracts will be based on “conditions of Contract for Construction for Building and Engineering Works Designed by the Employer (“Red book”) Second edition 2017” published by the Federation Internationale Des Ingenieurs - Conseils (FIDIC). The Contract Period will be 30 months from the commencement date for Lot 4 and 20 months from the commencement date for Lot 5. The Defects Notification Period will be 60 months.

Common Issues + FAQs

What does 'Availability Unknown' Mean?

Tenders.Net does not know of any electronic documentation or electronic tender boxes for this opportunity at this time. Please refer the tender contact details instead.

If any such information is later released, we will send out the advertisement again with the [Updated] flag.


I Get 'Page Not Found' when I click on a link.

Please make sure you have an active connection to the internet and have not used up your data limit. If your connection is fine then the linked site may be temporarily down. We mark any link that goes outside of Tenders.Net with an asterisk ( * ).

If the link continues to fail you should then use the tender contact details instead for assistance.


I can't log in to the 'Documentation' or 'Lodgement' links.

If you are being taken to an external site - the link will be marked with an asterisk ( * ) - then your Tenders.Net login details will not work. You will need another access to that site.

If you are asked to connect to www.tenders.net and do not have an access please contact us or register to get a login. If you cannot remember your access you can retrieve your password here.


What is a 'Selective' or 'Invitation Only' Tender?

Unlike public tendering where anyone can make a response, some opportunities are restricted only to those who have been directly invited. These are sometimes the result of a public EOI.

You will not normally be able to respond to these opportunities unless you have been invited.

Free Advertising

Empower your organisations procurement practices with Tenders.Net. By advertising your RFx opportunity on Tenders.Net you reach thousands of suppliers (like you!) through our dedicated notification system.

Designed to meet the requirements of Government, the system provides audit logs for your entire opportunity, dedicated cloud lodgement facilities, supplier forums and much more.
